info.title: Chronicle API
info.description: The Google Cloud Security Operations API (Chronicle API) provides endpoints that help analysts investigate and mitigate security threats throughout their lifecycle.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Optional. The maximum number of enrichment controls to return. The service may return
fewer than this value. If unspecified, at most 100 enrichment controls will
be returned. The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 will be coerced
to 1000.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
Optional. A page token, received from a previous `ListEnrichmentControls` call.
Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.

When paginating, all other parameters provided to `ListEnrichmentControls`
must match the call that provided the page token.

post.description:
Disable an EnrichmentControl. Disabling an enrichment control involves
updating the end time of the most recent record associated with that
EnrichmentControl resource to the disabling time and return the updated
EnrichmentControl resource.

AlertGroupingRule.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
AlertGroupingRule - Chronicle Alert Grouping Rule.
Types of AlertGroupingRules: general, case-spesific

category.description: Required. The category of the new stage to create.

groupingType.description: Required. Defines the AlertGroupingRule grouping_type in the lifetime of a case.

entityType.description: Required. AlertGroupingRule associated with the entity_type.

categoryDetails.description: Required. Defines the AlertGroupingRule category details list.

CategoryDetail.description: Represents details of a category within an AlertGroupingRule.

identifier.description: Required. Unique identifier for the category detail.

displayName.description: Required. Human-readable name for the category detail.

alertGroupingRules.description:
The list of AlertGroupingRules.
Ordered by Id by default.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description:
Total number of AlertGroupingRules includes filter without
pagination.

Announcement.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
Announcements - Chronicle SOAR Announcements.

updateTime.description: Output only. Last update time of the announcement.

content.description: Required. The announcement content, limited to 500 characters.

expireTime.description: Optional. The announcement expiration time, must be a future time.

title.description:
Required. The announcement title. The title must be at least 3 characters and limited
to 100 characters.

author.description: Output only. The user who created the announcement.

lastAuthor.description: Output only. The last user who edited the announcement.

nextPageToken.description:
A page token, received from a previous `ListAnnouncements` call.
Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page.

When paginating, all other parameters provided to `ListAnnouncements` must
match the call that provided the page token.

totalSize.description: Total number of Announcements.

Attachment.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
Represents a file uploaded to SecOps by a user. These files
provide supporting evidence, documentation, or relevant data for security
investigations and can be associated with various entities within the system.

fileName.description:
Output only. The file name including its file extension, maximum of 256
characters.

description.description: Optional. The attachment description, maximum of 100 characters.

mimeType.description: Output only. The attachment mime type, maximum of 100 characters.

author.description: Output only. The user who uploaded the attachment.

ListAttachmentsResponse.description: ListAttachmentsResponse contains the list of Attachments.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description: Optional. The total number of Attachments matching the request.

CreateAttachmentRequest.description: CreateAttachmentRequest contains the request to create an Attachment.

description.description: Required. The Attachment to create.

media.description: The media object to be uploaded.

Media.description: A reference to data stored on the filesystem, on GFS or in blobstore.

timestamp.description:
Time at which the media data was last updated,
in milliseconds since UNIX epoch

token.description: A unique fingerprint/version id for the media data

length.description: Size of the data, in bytes

referenceType.description: Describes what the field reference contains.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Reference contains a GFS path or a local path.

Reference points to a blobstore object. This could be either
a v1 blob_ref or a v2 blobstore2_info. Clients should check
blobstore2_info first, since v1 is being deprecated.
Data is included into this proto buffer

Data should be accessed from the current service
using the operation GetMedia.

The content for this media object is stored across
multiple partial media objects under the
composite_media field.
Reference points to a bigstore object
Indicates the data is stored in diff_version_response.
Indicates the data is stored in diff_checksums_response.
Indicates the data is stored in diff_download_response.
Indicates the data is stored in diff_upload_request.
Indicates the data is stored in diff_upload_response.
Indicates the data is stored in cosmo_binary_reference.

Informs Scotty to generate a response payload with the size specified
in the length field. The contents of the payload are generated by
Scotty and are undefined. This is useful for testing download speeds
between the user and Scotty without involving a real payload source.

Note: range is not supported when using arbitrary_bytes.

path.description: Path to the data, set if reference_type is PATH

blobRef.description:
Blobstore v1 reference, set if reference_type is BLOBSTORE_REF
This should be the byte representation of a blobstore.BlobRef.
Since Blobstore is deprecating v1, use blobstore2_info instead.
For now, any v2 blob will also be represented in this field as
v1 BlobRef.

inline.description: Media data, set if reference_type is INLINE

mediaId.description:
Media id to forward to the operation GetMedia.
Can be set if reference_type is GET_MEDIA.

hash.description:
Deprecated, use one of explicit hash type fields instead.
These two hash related fields will only be populated on Scotty based media
uploads and will contain the content of the hash group in the
NotificationRequest:
http://cs/#google3/blobstore2/api/scotty/service/proto/upload_listener.proto&q=class:Hash
Hex encoded hash value of the uploaded media.

algorithm.description:
Deprecated, use one of explicit hash type fields instead.
Algorithm used for calculating the hash.
As of 2011/01/21, "MD5" is the only possible value for this field.
New values may be added at any time.

compositeMedia.description:
A composite media composed of one or more media objects, set if
reference_type is COMPOSITE_MEDIA. The media length field must be set
to the sum of the lengths of all composite media objects.

Note: All composite media must have length specified.

objectId.description: Reference to a TI Blob, set if reference_type is BIGSTORE_REF.

blobstore2Info.description:
Blobstore v2 info, set if reference_type is BLOBSTORE_REF and it refers
to a v2 blob.

contentTypeInfo.description: Extended content type information provided for Scotty uploads.

crc32cHash.description:
For Scotty Uploads:
Scotty-provided hashes for uploads

For Scotty Downloads:
(WARNING: DO NOT USE WITHOUT PERMISSION FROM THE SCOTTY TEAM.)
A Hash provided by the agent to be used to verify the data being
downloaded. Currently only supported for inline payloads.
Further, only crc32c_hash is currently supported.

md5Hash.description: Scotty-provided MD5 hash for an upload.

sha1Hash.description: Scotty-provided SHA1 hash for an upload.

sha256Hash.description: Scotty-provided SHA256 hash for an upload.

isPotentialRetry.description:
|is_potential_retry| is set false only when Scotty is
certain that it has not sent the request before. When a client resumes
an upload, this field must be set true in agent calls, because Scotty
cannot be certain that it has never sent the request before due
to potential failure in the session state persistence.

cosmoBinaryReference.description:
A binary data reference for a media download. Serves as a
technology-agnostic binary reference in some Google infrastructure.
This value is a serialized storage_cosmo.BinaryReference proto. Storing
it as bytes is a hack to get around the fact that the cosmo proto
(as well as others it includes) doesn't support JavaScript. This
prevents us from including the actual type of this field.

hashVerified.description:
For Scotty uploads only. If a user sends a hash code and the backend has
requested that Scotty verify the upload against the client hash,
Scotty will perform the check on behalf of the backend and will reject it
if the hashes don't match. This is set to true if Scotty performed
this verification.

CompositeMedia.description:
A sequence of media data references representing composite data.
Introduced to support Bigstore composite objects. For details, visit
http://go/bigstore-composites.

length.description: Size of the data, in bytes

referenceType.description: Describes what the field reference contains.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Reference contains a GFS path or a local path.

Reference points to a blobstore object. This could be either
a v1 blob_ref or a v2 blobstore2_info. Clients should check
blobstore2_info first, since v1 is being deprecated.
Data is included into this proto buffer
Reference points to a bigstore object
Indicates the data is stored in cosmo_binary_reference.

path.description: Path to the data, set if reference_type is PATH

blobRef.description:
Blobstore v1 reference, set if reference_type is BLOBSTORE_REF
This should be the byte representation of a blobstore.BlobRef.
Since Blobstore is deprecating v1, use blobstore2_info instead.
For now, any v2 blob will also be represented in this field as
v1 BlobRef.

inline.description: Media data, set if reference_type is INLINE

objectId.description: Reference to a TI Blob, set if reference_type is BIGSTORE_REF.

blobstore2Info.description:
Blobstore v2 info, set if reference_type is BLOBSTORE_REF and it refers
to a v2 blob.

cosmoBinaryReference.description:
A binary data reference for a media download. Serves as a
technology-agnostic binary reference in some Google infrastructure.
This value is a serialized storage_cosmo.BinaryReference proto. Storing
it as bytes is a hack to get around the fact that the cosmo proto
(as well as others it includes) doesn't support JavaScript. This
prevents us from including the actual type of this field.

ObjectId.description:
This is a copy of the tech.blob.ObjectId proto, which could not
be used directly here due to transitive closure issues with
JavaScript support; see http://b/8801763.

bucketName.description: The name of the bucket to which this object belongs.

generation.description:
Generation of the object. Generations are monotonically increasing
across writes, allowing them to be be compared to determine which
generation is newer. If this is omitted in a request, then you are
requesting the live object.
See http://go/bigstore-versions

readToken.description:
The blob read token. Needed to read blobs that have not been
replicated. Might not be available until the final call.

uploadMetadataContainer.description:
Metadata passed from Blobstore -> Scotty for a new GCS upload.
This is a signed, serialized blobstore2.BlobMetadataContainer proto which
must never be consumed outside of Bigstore, and is not applicable to
non-GCS media uploads.

downloadReadHandle.description:
Read handle passed from Bigstore -> Scotty for a GCS download.
This is a signed, serialized blobstore2.ReadHandle proto which must never
be set outside of Bigstore, and is not applicable to non-GCS media
downloads.

downloadExternalReadToken.description:
A serialized External Read Token passed from Bigstore -> Scotty for a GCS
download. This field must never be consumed outside of Bigstore, and is not
applicable to non-GCS media uploads.

uploadFragmentListCreationInfo.description:
A serialized Object Fragment List Creation Info passed from Bigstore ->
Scotty for a GCS upload. This field must never be consumed outside of
Bigstore, and is not applicable to non-GCS media uploads.

DiffVersionResponse.description:
Backend response for a Diff get version response.
For details on the Scotty Diff protocol,
visit http://go/scotty-diff-protocol.

objectVersion.description: The version of the object stored at the server.

objectSizeBytes.description: The total size of the server object.

DiffChecksumsResponse.description:
Backend response for a Diff get checksums response.
For details on the Scotty Diff protocol,
visit http://go/scotty-diff-protocol.

objectVersion.description: The object version of the object the checksums are being returned for.

objectSizeBytes.description: The total size of the server object.

chunkSizeBytes.description: The chunk size of checksums.  Must be a multiple of 256KB.

checksumsLocation.description:
Exactly one of these fields must be populated.

If checksums_location is filled, the server will return the corresponding
contents to the user.  If object_location is filled, the server will
calculate the checksums based on the content there and return that to the
user.
For details on the format of the checksums,
see http://go/scotty-diff-protocol.

objectLocation.description:
If set, calculate the checksums based on the contents and return them to
the caller.

DiffDownloadResponse.description:
Backend response for a Diff download response.
For details on the Scotty Diff protocol,
visit http://go/scotty-diff-protocol.

DiffUploadRequest.description:
A Diff upload request.
For details on the Scotty Diff protocol,
visit http://go/scotty-diff-protocol.

objectVersion.description:
The object version of the object that is the base version the incoming
diff script will be applied to.
This field will always be filled in.

objectInfo.description:
The location of the new object.
Agents must clone the object located here, as the upload server will
delete the contents once a response is received.

checksumsInfo.description:
The location of the checksums for the new object.
Agents must clone the object located here, as the upload server will
delete the contents once a response is received.
For details on the format of the checksums,
see http://go/scotty-diff-protocol.

DiffUploadResponse.description:
Backend response for a Diff upload request.
For details on the Scotty Diff protocol,
visit http://go/scotty-diff-protocol.

objectVersion.description:
The object version of the object at the server. Must be included in the
end notification response.
The version in the end notification response must correspond to the new
version of the object that is now stored at the server, after the upload.

originalObject.description:
The location of the original file for a diff upload request. Must be
filled in if responding to an upload start notification.

ContentTypeInfo.description:
Detailed Content-Type information from Scotty. The Content-Type of the media
will typically be filled in by the header or Scotty's best_guess, but this
extended information provides the backend with more information so that it
can make a better decision if needed. This is only used on media upload
requests from Scotty.

bestGuess.description: Scotty's best guess of what the content type of the file is.

fromHeader.description:
The content type of the file as specified in the request headers,
multipart headers, or RUPIO start request.

fromFileName.description:
The content type of the file derived from the file extension of
the original file name used by the client.

fromUrlPath.description:
The content type of the file derived from the file extension of the
URL path.  The URL path is assumed to represent a file name (which
is typically only true for agents that are providing a REST API).

fromBytes.description:
The content type of the file derived by looking at specific
bytes (i.e. "magic bytes") of the actual file.

allowGzipCompression.description:
A boolean to be returned in the response to Scotty. Allows/disallows gzip
encoding of the payload content when the server thinks it's
advantageous (hence, does not guarantee compression) which allows
Scotty to GZip the response to the client.

ignoreRange.description:
Determining whether or not Apiary should skip the inclusion
of any Content-Range header on its response to Scotty.

MediaRequestInfo.description: Extra information added to operations that support Scotty media requests.

notificationType.description: The type of notification received from Scotty.
x-google-enum-descriptions:
Such requests signals the start of a request containing media
upload. Only the media field(s) in the inserted/updated resource
are set.

The response should either return an error or succeed. On success,
responses don't need to contain anything.

Such requests signals that the upload has progressed and that
the backend might want to access the media file specified in
relevant fields in the resource. Only the media field(s) in the
inserted/updated resource are set.

The response should either return an error or succeed. On success,
responses don't need to contain anything.

Such requests signals the end of a request containing media
upload. END should be handled just like normal Insert/Upload
requests, that is, they should process the request and return a
complete resource in the response.

Pointers to media data (a GFS path usually) appear in the
relevant fields in the inserted/updated resource. See
gdata.Media in data.proto.

Such requests occur after an END and signal that the response has
been sent back to the client. RESPONSE_SENT is only sent to
the backend if it is configured to receive them.

The response does not need to contain anything.

Such requests indicate that an error occurred while processing
the request. ERROR is only sent to the backend if it is configured
to receive them.  It is not guaranteed that all errors will result
in this notification to the backend, even if the backend requests
them.

Since these requests are just for informational purposes, the
response does not need to contain anything.

currentBytes.description: The number of current bytes uploaded or downloaded.

totalBytes.description: The total size of the file.

totalBytesIsEstimated.description: Whether the total bytes field contains an estimated data.

customData.description:
Data to be copied to backend requests. Custom data is returned to Scotty in
the agent_state field, which Scotty will then provide in subsequent upload
notifications.

diffObjectVersion.description:
Set if the http request info is diff encoded. The value of
this field is the version number of the base revision.
This is corresponding to Apiary's mediaDiffObjectVersion
(//depot/google3/java/com/google/api/server/media/variable/DiffObjectVersionVariable.java).
See go/esf-scotty-diff-upload for more information.

finalStatus.description:
The existence of the final_status field indicates that this is the last
call to the agent for this request_id.
http://google3/uploader/agent/scotty_agent.proto?l=737&rcl=347601929

requestReceivedParamsServingInfo.description:
The partition of the Scotty server handling this request.
type is uploader_service.RequestReceivedParamsServingInfo
LINT.IfChange(request_received_params_serving_info_annotations)
LINT.ThenChange()

physicalHeaders.description:
The physical headers provided by RequestReceivedParameters in
Scotty request. type is uploader_service.KeyValuePairs.

name.description: Output only. The created attachment.

fileName.description:
Output only. The file name including its file extension, maximum of 256
characters.

description.description: Output only. The attachment description, maximum of 100 characters.

mimeType.description: Output only. The attachment mime type, maximum of 100 characters.

author.description: Output only. The user who uploaded the attachment.

IntegrationInstance.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
Represents an instance of an Integration, including general configurations
for execution of Playbook Actions, Connectors & Jobs.

displayName.description:
Optional. The display name of the integration instance. Automatically generated
if not assigned. Limited to 110 characters.

description.description: Optional. The integration instance description. Limited to 1500 characters.

configured.description: Output only. Determines whether the integration instance is configured.

remote.description:
Output only. True if the integration instance is a remote instance.
Instance is created with remote = false by default.

agent.description: Optional. The agent identifier for a remote integration instance

systemDefault.description: Output only. Indicates whether the integration instance is the system default.

parameters.description: Optional. Integration's parameters.

identifier.description: Output only. The integration instance identifier.

IntegrationInstanceParameter.description:
Represents an instance of an Integration, including general configurations
for execution of Playbook Actions, Connectors & Jobs.

id.description: Output only. The unique id of the integration instance parameter.

description.description: Output only. The integration instance parameter description.

displayName.description: Output only. The integration instance parameter display name.

propertyName.description: Output only. The integration instance parameter property name.

type.description: Output only. The integration instance parameter type.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The type of the integration instance parameter is unknown.
The type of the integration instance parameter is boolean.
The type of the integration instance parameter is integer.
The type of the integration instance parameter is string.
The type of the integration instance parameter is password.
The type of the integration instance parameter is IP.
The type of the integration instance parameter is IP or host.
The type of the integration instance parameter is URL.
The type of the integration instance parameter is domain.
The type of the integration instance parameter is email.
The type of the integration instance parameter is values list.

The type of the integration instance parameter is values as semicolon
separated string.
The type of the integration instance parameter is multi values selection.
The type of the integration instance parameter is script.
The type of the integration instance parameter is filter list.

mandatory.description:
Output only. Indicates if the parameter is required when configuring the integration
instance.

nextPageToken.description:
Optional. A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description: The total number of IntegrationInstances.

ExecuteIntegrationInstanceTestRequest.description: Test the integration instance (execute the “ping” action).

successful.description: Output only. Indicates if the test was successful.

instance.description: Output only. The integration default instance.

playbooks.description: Output only. List of affected playbooks.

displayName.description: Required. The playbook name.

IntegrationAction.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
This resource describes an action definition as part of an integration.

displayName.description: Required. Action's definition name, limited to a maximum of 150 characters.

description.description: Optional. Action's description, limited to a maximum or 400 characters.

script.description: Required. Action's script, limited to a maximum size of 5MB.

author.description: Output only. User that created the action in the system.

enabled.description: Required. Determines whether the action is enabled or disabled.

scriptResultName.description:
Required. The script result name.
Describes the field name that holds the script result.
Has a maximum character limit of 100 characters.

timeoutSeconds.description:
Required. Action timeout in seconds.
The maximum allowed value is 1200 seconds.

async.description: Required. Determines whether the action is async or not.

asyncPollingIntervalSeconds.description:
Optional. The async polling interval time in seconds.
The maximum allowed value can not exceed total timeout secs.
Relevant only for async actions

asyncTotalTimeoutSeconds.description:
Optional. The total async polling interval timeout in seconds.
The maximum allowed value is 14(days)x24x3600 = 1209600 secs.
Relevant only for async actions

defaultResultValue.description:
Optional. Action's default result value.
Has a maximum character limit of 1000 characters.

widgetTemplateIdentifier.description:
Output only. The widget template identifier.
Relevant only for actions which contain a widget.

dynamicResults.description:
Optional. List of dynamic results metadata.
The max count for the items is 50.

parameters.description:
Optional. Action's parameters.
The max count for the items is 50.

integration.description: Output only. The parent integration identifier.

custom.description: Output only. Determines whether the action is custom or not.

hasJsonResult.description: Output only. Describes whether the action has a json result.

dynamicResultsMetadataJson.description: Output only. The dynamic results metadata json.

id.description: Output only. The action id.

aiGenerated.description: Optional. Determines whether the action was generated by AI or not.

aiCategories.description: Output only. The categories of the action, generated by AI.

aiDescription.description: Output only. The description of the action, generated by AI.

entityTypes.description:
Output only. The entity types that the action can run on. For example, if it's an action
that runs on an IP (like EnrichIP), this will be ["ADDRESS"].

resultExample.description:
Optional. The result example.
Has a maximum character limit of 100000 characters.

displayResult.description: Required. Determines whether to display the result.

ActionParameter.description:
Action parameter object.
Action may include parameters that will be filled and during the execution.

id.description: Output only. The parameter's id.

mandatory.description: Required. Describes whether the parameter is mandatory.

defaultValue.description:
Optional. The default value of the parameter.
Has a maximum character limit of 150 characters.

displayName.description:
Required. The parameter's display name.
Has a maximum character limit of 150 characters.

type.description: Required. The parameter's type.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Close case root cause repository type.

description.description:
Required. The parameter's description.
Has a maximum character limit of 150 characters.

optionalValues.description:
Optional. Parameter's optional values.
The max count for the items is 50.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description: Output only. Total number of IntegrationActions.

actions.description:
Required. List of actions that have integration instances in one of the given
environments.

template.description: Output only. The action definition template.

integrationInstanceId.description: Required. The integration instance id to use.

action.description: Required. The action which will be tested.

resultJson.description: Optional. The result json if exists.

View.description:
View - Chronicle View.
Types of Views: general, case-specific

displayName.description: Output only. Defines the view display name.

identifier.description: Output only. The identifier of the view.

type.description: Optional. Defines the view type.
x-google-enum-descriptions:
Case overview view type V2.
This is an updated version of CASE_OVERVIEW for the Cases2.0 UI.

Alert overview view type V2. This is an updated version of
ALERT_OVERVIEW for the Cases2.0 UI.

widgets.description: Optional. Defines the widgets in the view.

firstPlaybookRunTime.description:
Output only. If this view is associated with a playbook and an alert, this will be
populated with the time the playbook was first run on the alert. Used
to determine the order of views in the UI.
NOTE: Uses Unix epoch time in milliseconds to match the SOAR return type.

id.description: Optional. Unique numeric ID of the view.

TemplateWidgetDefinition.description: TemplateWidgetDefinition - Chronicle soar Template Widget Definition.

metadata.description: Required. Metadata of the template widget.

config.description: Optional. Config is the configuration of the widget as a JSON string.

CaseAlert.description:
Security notifications tied to specific data identified as a potential
threat by security systems. In SecOps, investigating alerts provides
critical context about the underlying activity and its relationships to
entities and other security events.

identifier.description:
Output only. Title + Guid, e.g. ACCESS DISABLED
ACCOUNTS_3A0C90F9-A87E-4E94-8727-7884F686ECDA
Legacy identifier of alert used across the legacy modules in the system.
Char limit: 2100

caseId.description: Output only. Case associated with the alert. Added for convenience to be used by the UI.

createTime.description: Output only. The creation time of the record in milliseconds.

updateTime.description: Output only. The modification time of the record in milliseconds.

ruleGenerator.description:
Output only. Which rule triggered the alert on the third party. This can be any rule
defined in the third party Such SIEM, Splunk, CrowdStrike. Characters
limit: 250.

sourceGroupingIdentifier.description:
Output only. A key on the alert that can be used to group alerts to the same case.
Characters limit: 2100

product.description:
Output only. The product associated with the alert.
E.g. DLP, WinEventLog:Security

displayName.description:
Output only. The display name of the alert.
E.g. "DATA EXFILTRATION"

vendor.description:
Output only. The vendor of the alert.
E.g. "Microsoft". Characters limit: 2100

environment.description: Output only. The environment of the alert.

ticketId.description:
Output only. Third party ticket id, can be originated from SIEM or other tools.
E.g. "3a0c90f9-a87e-4e94-8727-7884f686ecda"

sourceSystemName.description:
Output only. Which alerting system raises the alert.
E.g. "QRadar", "Arcsight", "Microsoft CASB".
The Integration Name in soar.

closureDetails.description: Optional. Defines the close reason of an alert if any.

sla.description:
Optional. SLA (Service Level Agreement) for the specific alert, used also to
calculate aggregate case sla.

manual.description: Output only. Whether the alert was created manually.

priority.description: Optional. Default value is HIGH.

sourceIdentifier.description:
Output only. Stores the identifier of the source of the alert, could be a connector
identifier etc.

additionalProperties.description:
Output only. Stores additional data on specific alerts, currently used by connectors, in
JSON format.

status.description:
Optional. Alert status.
Default value is OPEN.

startTime.description: Output only. When the alert was created on the third party product (SIEM, IPS, etc).

endTime.description: Output only. When the alert was closed on the third party product (SIEM, IPS, etc).

involvedRelations.description:
Output only. All involved relations for the alert.
Directional connection between entities in a given alert.

siemAlertId.description: Output only. The identifier of the alert int SIEM.

sourceUrl.description: Output only. The source URL of the alert.

sourceRuleUrl.description: Output only. The source rule URL of the alert.

sourceSystemUrl.description: Output only. The source system URL.

sourceRuleIdentifier.description: Output only. The source rule identifier.

attachedPlaybookName.description: Output only. The attached playbook name.

nestingDepth.description: Output only. The nesting depth of the alert.

alertGroupIdentifier.description: Output only. The alert group identifier.

eventCount.description: Output only. The number of events that triggered the alert.

playbookRunCount.description: Output only. The number of times the first playbook was run on the alert.

tags.description: Optional. AlertTags associated with the alert.

rootCause.description: Output only. Alert closure root cause.

closingTimeMs.description: Output only. Alert closure time in unix format as milliseconds.

Sla.description: SLA for the case or alert.

expirationTime.description:
Required. SLA expiration time in unix format as milliseconds. Old prop:
SlaExpiration.

criticalExpirationTime.description:
Required. SLA critical expiration time in unix format as milliseconds, old prop:
SlaCriticalExpiration.

x-google-enum-descriptions: SLA is critical and has expired.

remainingTimeSinceLastPause.description: Output only. Remaining time since last pause.

CreateWithAttachmentResponse.description: Response model for create a chat-message with an attachment operation

MediaResponseInfo.description:
This message is for backends to pass their scotty media specific fields to
ESF. Backend will include this in their response message to ESF.
Example:
 ExportFile is an rpc defined for upload using scotty from ESF.

  rpc ExportFile(ExportFileRequest) returns (ExportFileResponse)

Message ExportFileResponse will include apiserving.MediaResponseInfo to tell
ESF about data like dynamic_dropzone it needs to pass to Scotty.

  message ExportFileResponse {
   optional gdata.Media blob = 1;
   optional apiserving.MediaResponseInfo media_response_info = 2
 }

dynamicDropzone.description: Specifies the Scotty dropzone to use for uploads.

customData.description:
Data to copy from backend response to the next backend requests. Custom
data is returned to Scotty in the agent_state field, which Scotty will then
provide in subsequent upload notifications.

dynamicDropTarget.description:
Specifies the Scotty Drop Target to use for uploads. If present in a media
response, Scotty does not upload to a standard drop zone. Instead, Scotty
saves the upload directly to the location specified in this drop target.
Unlike drop zones, the drop target is the final storage location for an
upload. So, the agent does not need to clone the blob at the end of the
upload. The agent is responsible for garbage collecting any orphaned blobs
that may occur due to aborted uploads. For more information, see the drop
target design doc here: http://goto/ScottyDropTarget This field will be
preferred to dynamicDropzone. If provided, the identified field in the
response must be of the type uploader.agent.DropTarget.

dataStorageTransform.description:
Specifies any transformation to be applied to data before persisting it or
retrieving from storage. E.g., encryption options for blobstore2.
This should be of the form uploader_service.DataStorageTransform.

trafficClassField.description:
Specifies the TrafficClass that Scotty should use for any RPCs to fetch the
response bytes. Will override the traffic class GTOS of the incoming http
request. This is a temporary field to facilitate whitelisting and
experimentation by the bigstore agent only. For instance, this does not
apply to RTMP reads.
WARNING: DO NOT USE WITHOUT PERMISSION FROM THE SCOTTY TEAM.

mediaForDiff.description:
Diff Updates must respond to a START notification with this Media proto to
tell Scotty to decode the diff encoded payload and apply the diff against
this field.
If the request was diff encoded, but this field is not set, Scotty will
treat the encoding as identity.
This is corresponding to Apiary's DiffUploadResponse.original_object
(//depot/google3/gdata/rosy/proto/data.proto?l=413).
See go/esf-scotty-diff-upload for more information.

verifyHashFromHeader.description:
Tells Scotty to verify hashes on the agent's behalf by parsing out the
X-Goog-Hash header.

requestClass.description: Request class to use for all Blobstore operations for this request.

scottyCustomerLog.description:
Customer-specific data to be recorded in the Scotty logs
type is logs_proto_scotty.CustomerLog

scottyAgentUserId.description: Requester ID passed along to be recorded in the Scotty logs

originalObjectBlobMintIndex.description:
For the first notification of a |diff_encoded| HttpRequestInfo, this is the
index of the blob mint that Scotty should use when reading the original
blob. This field is optional. It's not required ever, even if
`destination_blob_mint_index` is set. In situations like that, we will use
the destination blob's mint for the destination blob and regular blob ACL
checks for the original object.

Note: This field is only for use by Drive API for diff uploads.

destinationBlobMintIndex.description:
For the first notification of a |diff_encoded| HttpRequestInfo, this is the
index of the blob mint that Scotty should use when writing the resulting
blob. This field is optional. It's not required ever, even if
`original_object_blob_mint_index` is set. In situations like that, we will
use the destination blob's mint for the destination blob and regular blob
ACL checks for the original object.

Note: This field is only for use by Drive API for diff uploads.

Ioc.description:
Represents an Ioc (Indicator of Compromise) which is flagged as suscpicious
by customer's security infrastructure or by IoC partners of Google. Each Ioc
comes from a single source feed so there can be multiple Iocs with same Ioc
value.

name.description: Identifier. The resource name of this IndicatorOfCompromise (Ioc).

categorization.description:
The category/type of this indicator.
Ex: "Spyware", "Bitcoin_Related", etc.

description.description: Raw description of the IoC.

sourceFeed.description: Original Feed name from where the indicator originated.

artifactIndicator.description:
Optional. An indicator to an artifact. An artifact is an identifier like a website
domain, file hash, or IP address that is used to identify a malicious
website, file, or computer system that might be contacted or used by a
device in customer's environment.

priority.description:
Optional. Priority of an IoC. Priority is determined from GCTI rules based on the
indicator and ingested event.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The default value of the priority
Ioc match priority is ACTIVE BREACH

displayName.description:
Immutable. Value of an IOC which can be either a domain, ip address or file hash (MD5,
SHA1, SHA256).

iocIngestionTime.description:
Timestamp when the IoC was first received from ANY feed. This is the
earliest timestamp of receipt by Malachite, given that the artifact might
have been sent in multiple feeds at different times.

discoveryTime.description: First time the ioc was discovered by provider

lastUpdateTime.description: Last time the IoC was seen by mandiant

associations.description:
Associations represents different metadata about malware and threat actors
involved with an IoC.

verdictInfo.description:
Verdict information about the IoC from the provider.
Describes the threat verdict provided by human analysts and machine
learning models.

globalCustomerId.description: Optional. Global customer id of an IOC

confidenceScore.description:
IoC confidence score. Raw confidence level from the IoC source, higher
score means more confidence. If there is no confidence score in the
raw data, this will not be set.

confidenceBucket.description: IoC confidence score, bucketed into e.g. low/medium/high.

campaigns.description: List of campaigns this IoC was observed.

gtiThreatScore.description: Output only. Google Threat Intelligence threat score of the IoC.

IocArtifactIndicator.description:
An indicator to identify an artifact.
Artifact indicator can be one of domain name, destination ip address,
md5 hash, sha1 hash, or sha256 hash.

IocAssociation.description:
Associations represents different metadata about malware and threat actors
involved with an IoC.

id.description:
Output only. Association identifier. This can be used to request details of the
IocAssociation from the Google Threat Intelligence API
(https://gtidocs.virustotal.com/reference/get-threat)

countryCode.description:
Output only. Deprecated. Use region_code instead.
Country from which the threat actor originated.

regionCode.description: Output only. Name of the country the threat is originating from.

type.description: Output only. Signifies the type of association.

threatDisplayName.description: Output only. Name of the threat actor/malware.

description.description: Output only. Human readable description about the association.

alias.description: Output only. Different aliases of the threat actor given by different sources.

firstReferenceTime.description: Output only. First time the threat actor was referenced or seen.

lastReferenceTime.description: Output only. Last time the threat actor was referenced or seen.

industriesAffected.description: Output only. List of industries the threat actor affects.

associatedActors.description:
Output only. List of associated threat actors for a malware.
Not applicable for threat actors.

roles.description:
Output only. List of roles associated with the malware. Not applicable for threat
actors.

operatingSystems.description:
Output only. List of operating systems that associated with the malware. Not applicable
for threat actors.

location.description:
Custom location name (e.g. building or site name like "London Office").
For cloud environments, this is the region (e.g. "us-west2").

desk.description:
Desk name or individual location, typically for an employee in an
office.
(e.g. "IN-BLR-BCPC-11-1121D").

floor.description:
Floor name, number or a combination of the two for a building.
(e.g. "1-A").

LatLng.description:
An object that represents a latitude/longitude pair. This is expressed as a
pair of doubles to represent degrees latitude and degrees longitude. Unless
specified otherwise, this object must conform to the
WGS84 standard. Values must be within normalized ranges.

latitude.description: The latitude in degrees. It must be in the range [-90.0, +90.0].

longitude.description: The longitude in degrees. It must be in the range [-180.0, +180.0].

IocAssociationAlias.description: Association Alias  Used to represent Mandiant Threat intel.

associationName.description: Output only. Name of the alias.

provider.description: Output only. Name of the provider who gave the association's name.

IocVerdictInfo.description:
Describes the threat verdict provided by human analysts and machine
learning models. These fields are used to model Mandiant sources.

sourceCount.description: Number of sources from which intel was extracted.

responseCount.description: Total response count across all sources.

neighbourInfluence.description: Describes the near neighbor influence of the verdict.

x-google-enum-descriptions:
MLVerdict result provided from threat providers, like Mandiant. These
fields are used to model Mandiant sources.

Verdict provided by the human analyst. These fields are used to model
Mandiant sources.

sourceProvider.description: Source provider giving the machine learning verdict.

benignCount.description: Count of responses where this IoC was marked as benign.

maliciousCount.description: Count of responses where this IoC was marked as malicious.

confidenceScore.description: Confidence score of the verdict. Range of Confidence Score is 0-100.

iocStats.description: List of IocStats from which the verdict was generated.

verdictTime.description: Timestamp when the verdict was generated.

x-google-enum-descriptions: VerdictResponse resulted a threat as malicious.
VerdictResponse resulted a threat as benign.

globalCustomerCount.description: Global customer count over the last 30 days

globalHitsCount.description: Global hit count over the last 30 days.

pwn.description:
Whether one or more Mandiant incident response customers had this
indicator in their environment.

pwnFirstTaggedTime.description: The timestamp of the first time a pwn was associated to this entity.

IocStats.description:
Information about the threat intel source. These fields are used to
model Mandiant sources.

iocStatsType.description: Describes the source of the IoCStat.
x-google-enum-descriptions: IoCStat is from a Mandiant Source.
IoCStat is from a third-party source.
IoCStat is from a threat intelligence feed.

firstLevelSource.description: Name of first level IoC source, for example Mandiant or a third-party.

secondLevelSource.description:
Name of the second-level IoC source, for example Crowdsourced Threat
Analysis or Knowledge Graph.

benignCount.description: Count of responses where the IoC was identified as benign.

quality.description: Level of confidence in the IoC mapping extracted from the source.

maliciousCount.description: Count of responses where the IoC was identified as malicious.

responseCount.description: Total number of response from the source.

sourceCount.description: Number of sources from which information was extracted.
