FindIocsRequest.description: A request to find a list of Iocs with specified types and indicators.

fieldAndValue.description:
Required. Parameters to identify the IOCs. Each item should uniquely identify one Ioc
record. Only `value_type` is supported, and only the following ValueType
options:
-  HASH_MD5
-  HASH_SHA1
-  HASH_SHA256
-  DOMAIN_NAME
-  RESOLVED_IP_ADDRESS

GoogleCloudChronicleCommonFieldAndValue.description: Indicator value with field path to identity an entity.

value.description: Required. Indicator value that is used to identify or find the entity.

fieldPath.description:
A UDM field path which identifies the type of the indicator to be used
to find the entity.
This path is not used exclusively to "search" for the entity, but rather
to identify the type of indicator, which can be inferred from the path.

valueType.description: An explicit type of the indicator to be used to find the entity.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Asset product id. Product specific ID for EDR/HIDS/AV products, etc.
Process id for the parent that spawned a process.
User product object id. Product specific object ID for LDAP-like systems.

entityNamespace.description:
Optional. Entity namespace.
Namespace is only applicable to asset entities.

FindIocsResponse.description: The response to a FindIocs request.

iocs.description:
The Iocs that match the request.
Note this returns a max of 1000 IOCs, in the order requested.

Entity.description:
An Entity provides additional context about an entity in a UDM event (asset,
user, etc.). For example, a PROCESS_LAUNCH event describes that user
'abc@example.corp' launched process 'shady.exe'. The event does not include
information that user 'abc@example.com' is a recently terminated employee who
administers a server storing finance data. Information stored in one or more
Entities can add this additional context.

name.description:
The resource name of the entity.
Format:
`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/entities/{entity}`
`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/analytics/{analytic}/entities/{entity}`
`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/watchlists/{watchlist}/entities/{entity}`

metadata.description: Entity metadata such as timestamp, product, etc.

entity.description: Noun in the UDM event that this entity represents.

additional.description:
Important entity data that cannot be adequately represented within
the formal sections of the Entity.

riskScore.description: Represents the entity risk scores resource

metric.description: Metric details of the entity. Used if EntityType is METRIC.

relations.description:
One or more relationships between the entity (a) and other entities,
including the relationship type and related entity.

EntityMetadata.description:
Information about the Entity and the product where the entity was created.
Next Tag: 18

productEntityId.description:
A vendor-specific identifier that uniquely identifies the entity
(e.g. a GUID, LDAP, OID, or similar).

collectedTimestamp.description:
GMT timestamp when the entity information was collected by the vendor's
local collection infrastructure.

creationTimestamp.description:
GMT timestamp when the entity described by the product_entity_id was
created on the system where data was collected.

interval.description:
Valid existence time range for the version of the entity represented by
this entity data.

vendorName.description: Vendor name of the product that produced the entity information.

productName.description: Product name that produced the entity information.

feed.description: Vendor feed name for a threat indicator feed.

productVersion.description: Version of the product that produced the entity information.

entityType.description:
Entity type.
If an entity has multiple possible types, this specifies the most specific
type.
x-google-enum-descriptions: An asset, such as workstation, laptop, phone, virtual machine, etc.

description.description: Human-readable description of the entity.

threat.description:
Metadata provided by a threat intelligence feed that identified the
entity as malicious.

x-google-enum-descriptions: Entities ingested from customers (e.g. AD_CONTEXT, DLP_CONTEXT)

Entities derived from customer data such as prevalence, artifact
first/last seen, or asset/user first seen stats.
Global contextual entities such as WHOIS or Safe Browsing.

structuredFields.description: Structured fields extracted from the log.

extracted.description: Flattened fields extracted from the log.

atiPrioritization.description: Prioritization factors used by ATI curated rules.

Interval.description:
Represents a time interval, encoded as a Timestamp start (inclusive) and a
Timestamp end (exclusive).

The start must be less than or equal to the end.
When the start equals the end, the interval is empty (matches no time).
When both start and end are unspecified, the interval matches any time.

startTime.description:
Optional. Inclusive start of the interval.

If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be the same
or after the start.

endTime.description:
Optional. Exclusive end of the interval.

If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be before the
end.

SecurityResult.description:
Security related metadata for the event. A security result might be something
like "virus detected and quarantined," "malicious connection blocked," or
"sensitive data included in document foo.doc." Each security result, of which
there may be more than one, may either pertain to the whole event, or to a
specific object or device referenced in the event (e.g. a malicious file
that was detected, or a sensitive document sent as an email attachment). For
security results that apply to a particular object referenced in the event,
the security_results message MUST contain details about the implicated object
(such as process, user, IP, domain, URL, IP, or email address) in the about
field. For security results that apply to the entire event (e.g. SPAM found
in this email), the about field must remain empty.

about.description:
If the security result is about a specific entity (Noun), add it here.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

category.description:
The security category.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

x-google-enum-descriptions: Below the conviction threshold; probably bad.
Suspicious activity, such as potential reverse tunnel.
Non-security related: URL has category like gambling or porn.
Port scan detected by an IDS, probing of web app.
If we know this is a C&C channel.

Unauthorized access attempted, including attempted access to files,
web services, processes, web objects, etc.
Authentication failed (e.g. bad password or bad 2-factor authentication).

Exploit: For all manner of exploits including attempted overflows, bad
protocol encodings, ROP, SQL injection, etc. For both network and host-
based exploits.
DLP: Sensitive data transmission, copy to thumb drive.
DLP: Sensitive data found at rest in a scan.

Security-related policy violation (e.g. firewall/proxy/HIPS rule
violated, NAC block action).
Threats which manipulate to break normal security procedures.
Phishing pages, pops, https phishing etc.

categoryDetails.description:
For vendor-specific categories. For web categorization, put type in here
such as "gambling" or "porn".
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

threatName.description:
A vendor-assigned classification common across multiple customers
(for example, "W32/File-A", "Slammer").
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

ruleSet.description:
The curated detection's rule set identifier.
(for example, "windows-threats")
This is primarily set in rule-generated detections and alerts.

ruleSetDisplayName.description:
The curated detections rule set display name.
This is primarily set in rule-generated detections and alerts.

rulesetCategoryDisplayName.description:
The curated detection rule set category display name.
(for example, if rule_set_display_name is "CDIR SCC Enhanced Exfiltration",
the rule_set_category is "Cloud Threats").
This is primarily set in rule-generated detections and alerts.

ruleId.description:
A vendor-specific ID for a rule, varying by observer type
(e.g. "08123", "5d2b44d0-5ef6-40f5-a704-47d61d3babbe").

displayName.description:
The display name of the security result.
This is populated from 'name_override' Outcome Variable, if present.
Otherwise, this field is not set.

ruleVersion.description:
Version of the security rule.
(e.g. "v1.1", "00001", "1604709794", "2020-11-16T23:04:19+00:00").
Note that rule versions are source-dependant and lexical ordering
should not be assumed.

ruleAuthor.description:
Author of the security rule.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

ruleLabels.description:
A list of rule labels that can't be captured by the other fields
in security result
(e.g. "reference : AnotherRule", "contributor : John").
This is primarily set in rule-generated detections and alerts.

alertState.description:
The alerting types of this security result.
This is primarily set for rule-generated detections and alerts.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The security result type is not known.
The security result is not an alert.
The security result is an alert.

detectionFields.description:
An ordered list of values, that represent fields in detections for a
security finding. This list represents mapping of names of requested
entities to their values (the security result matched variables).

For Collection SecurityResults, prefer variables instead.

outcomes.description:
A list of outcomes that represent the results of this security finding.
This list represents a mapping of names of the requested outcomes,
to a stringified version of their values.

This is only populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.
This is deprecated. Use variables instead.

variables.description:
A list of outcomes and match variables that represent the results of this
security finding. This list represents a mapping of names of the requested
outcomes or match variables, to their values.

This is only populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

summary.description: A short human-readable summary (e.g. "failed login occurred")

description.description:
A human-readable description (e.g. "user password was wrong").
This can be more detailed than the summary.

action.description:
Actions taken for this event.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

x-google-enum-descriptions:
Strip, modify something
(e.g. File or email was disinfected or rewritten and still forwarded).
Put somewhere for later analysis (does NOT imply block).
Failed (e.g. the event was allowed but failed).
Challenged (e.g. the user was challenged by a Captcha, 2FA).

actionDetails.description:
The detail of the action taken as provided by the vendor.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

confidence.description:
The confidence level of the result as estimated by the product.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

priority.description:
The priority of the result.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

riskScore.description: The risk score of the security result.

confidenceScore.description:
The confidence score of the security result.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

analyticsMetadata.description:
Stores metadata about each risk analytic metric the rule uses.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

severityDetails.description:
Vendor-specific severity.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

confidenceDetails.description:
Additional detail with regards to the confidence of a security event as
estimated by the product vendor.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

priorityDetails.description:
Vendor-specific information about the security result priority.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

urlBackToProduct.description:
URL that takes the user to the source product console for this event.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

threatId.description:
Vendor-specific ID for a threat.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

threatFeedName.description:
Vendor feed name for a threat indicator feed.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

threatIdNamespace.description:
The attribute threat_id_namespace qualifies threat_id with an id namespace
to get an
unique id. The attribute threat_id by itself is not unique across Chronicle
as it is a vendor specific id.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.
x-google-enum-descriptions: A normalized telemetry event from Google Security Command Center.
An alert coming from other SIEMs via Chronicle SOAR.

threatStatus.description:
Current status of the threat
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

attackDetails.description:
MITRE ATT&CK details.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

firstDiscoveredTime.description:
First time the IoC threat was discovered in the provider.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

campaigns.description:
Campaigns using this IOC threat.
This is deprecated. Use threat_collections instead.

reports.description:
Reports that reference this IOC threat. These are the report IDs.
This is deprecated. Use threat_collections instead.

verdict.description:
Verdict about the IoC from the provider.
This field is now deprecated. Use VerdictInfo instead.

lastUpdatedTime.description:
Last time the IoC threat was updated in the provider.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

verdictInfo.description:
Verdict information about the IoC from the provider.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

threatVerdict.description:
GCTI threat verdict on the security result entity.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

lastDiscoveredTime.description:
Last time the IoC was seen in the provider data.
This field is not populated when the SecurityResult appears in a detection.

detectionDepth.description:
The depth of the detection chain.
Applies only to composite detections.

threatCollections.description: GTI collections associated with the security result.

Noun.description:
The Noun type is used to represent the different entities in an event:
principal, src, target, observer, intermediary, and about. It stores
attributes known about the entity. For example, if the entity is a device
with multiple IP or MAC addresses, it stores the IP and MAC addresses that
are relevant to the event.

hostname.description:
Client hostname or domain name field.
Hostname also doubles as the domain for remote entities.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for asset entities.

browser.description: Information about an entry in the web browser's local history database.

assetId.description:
The asset ID.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for asset entities.

userManagementChain.description:
Information about the user's management chain (reporting hierarchy).
Note: user_management_chain is only populated when data is exported to
BigQuery since recursive fields (e.g. user.managers) are not supported by
BigQuery.

processAncestors.description:
Information about the process's ancestors ordered from immediate ancestor
(parent process) to root.
Note: process_ancestors is only populated when data is exported to BigQuery
since recursive fields (e.g. process.parent_process) are not supported by
BigQuery.

ip.description:
A list of IP addresses associated with a network connection.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for asset entities.

natIp.description: A list of NAT translated IP addresses associated with a network connection.

port.description:
Source or destination network port number when a specific network
connection is described within an event.

natPort.description:
NAT external network port number when a specific network connection is
described within an event.

mac.description:
List of MAC addresses associated with a device.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for asset entities.

administrativeDomain.description:
Domain which the device belongs to (for example, the Microsoft Windows
domain).

namespace.description:
Namespace which the device belongs to, such as "AD forest".
Uses for this field include Microsoft Windows AD forest, the name of
subsidiary, or the name of acquisition.
This field can be used along with an asset indicator to identify an asset.

email.description:
Email address.
Only filled in for security_result.about

application.description:
The name of an application or service.
Some SSO solutions only capture the name of a target application
such as "Atlassian" or "Chronicle".

cloud.description:
Cloud metadata.
Deprecated: cloud should be populated in entity Attribute as generic
metadata (e.g. asset.attribute.cloud).

location.description:
Physical location. For cloud environments, set the region in
location.name.

ipGeoArtifact.description:
Enriched geographic information corresponding to an IP address.
Specifically, location and network data.

resource.description:
Information about the resource (e.g. scheduled task, calendar entry).
This field should not be used for files, registry, or processes because
these objects are already part of Noun.

resourceAncestors.description:
Information about the resource's ancestors ordered from immediate ancestor
(starting with parent resource).

labels.description:
Labels are key-value pairs.
For example: key = "env", value = "prod".
Deprecated: labels should be populated in entity Attribute as generic
metadata (e.g. user.attribute.labels).

objectReference.description: Finding to which the Analyst updated the feedback.

network.description:
Network details, including sub-messages with details on each protocol
(for example, DHCP, DNS, or HTTP).

name.description:
The domain name.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for Domain entities.

prevalence.description: The prevalence of the domain within the customer's environment.

firstSeenTime.description: First seen timestamp of the domain in the customer's environment.

lastSeenTime.description: Last seen timestamp of the domain in the customer's environment.

registrar.description:
Registrar name . FOr example, "Wild West Domains, Inc. (R120-LROR)",
"GoDaddy.com, LLC", or "PDR LTD. D/B/A PUBLICDOMAINREGISTRY.COM".

updateTime.description: Last updated time.

status.description:
Domain status. Seehttps://www.icann.org/resources/pages/epp-status-codes-2014-06-16-en
for meanings of possible values

registrant.description: Parsed contact information for the registrant of the domain.

admin.description: Parsed contact information for the administrative contact for the domain.

tech.description: Parsed contact information for the technical contact for the domain

billing.description: Parsed contact information for the billing contact of the domain.

zone.description: Parsed contact information for the zone.

privateRegistration.description:
Indicates whether the domain appears to be using a private registration
service to mask the owner's contact information.

categories.description: Categories assign to the domain as retrieved from VirusTotal.

favicon.description: Includes difference hash and MD5 hash of the domain's favicon.

lastDnsRecords.description: Domain's DNS records from the last scan.

lastDnsRecordsTime.description: Date when the DNS records list was retrieved by VirusTotal.

lastHttpsCertificate.description: SSL certificate object retrieved last time the domain was analyzed.

lastHttpsCertificateTime.description: When the certificate was retrieved by VirusTotal.

popularityRanks.description:
Domain's position in popularity ranks such as Alexa, Quantcast, Statvoo,
etc

whoisTime.description: Date of the last update of the WHOIS record.

Prevalence.description:
The prevalence of a resource within the customer's environment.
This measures how common it is for assets to access the resource.

rollingMax.description:
The maximum number of assets per day accessing the resource over the
trailing day_count days.

dayCount.description: The number of days over which rolling_max is calculated.

rollingMaxSubDomains.description:
The maximum number of assets per day accessing the domain along with
sub-domains over the trailing day_count days. This field is only valid for
domains.

dayMax.description: The max prevalence score in a day interval window.

dayMaxSubDomains.description:
The max prevalence score in a day interval window across sub-domains. This
field is only valid for domains.

productObjectId.description:
A vendor-specific identifier to uniquely identify the entity (e.g. a GUID,
LDAP, OID, or similar).
This field can be used as an entity indicator for user entities.

userid.description:
The ID of the user.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for user entities.

userDisplayName.description:
The display name of the user
(e.g. "John Locke").

attribute.description: Generic entity metadata attributes of the user.

firstSeenTime.description:
The first observed time for a user.
The value is calculated on the basis of the
first time the identifier was observed.

accountType.description:
Type of user account (for example, service, domain, or cloud). This is
somewhat aligned to: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078/
x-google-enum-descriptions: A human account part of some domain in directory services.
A SaaS service account type (such as Slack or GitHub).
A non-human account for data access.
A system built in default account.

groupid.description:
The ID of the group that the user belongs to.
Deprecated in favor of the repeated group_identifiers field.

groupIdentifiers.description:
Product object identifiers of the group(s) the user belongs to
A vendor-specific identifier to uniquely identify the group(s) the user
belongs to (a GUID, LDAP OID, or similar).

windowsSid.description:
The Microsoft Windows SID of the user.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for user entities.

emailAddresses.description:
Email addresses of the user.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for user entities.

employeeId.description:
Human capital management identifier.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for user entities.

timeOff.description: User time off leaves from active work.

lastBadPasswordAttemptTime.description: User last bad password attempt timestamp.

x-google-enum-descriptions: The authentication method is in active state.
The authentication method is in suspended/disabled state.
The authentication method has no active credentials.
The authentication method has been deleted.

roleName.description:
System role name for user.
Deprecated: use attribute.roles.

roleDescription.description:
System role description for user.
Deprecated: use attribute.roles.

userRole.description:
System role for user.
Deprecated: use attribute.roles.
x-google-enum-descriptions:
System service account for automated privilege access.
Deprecated: not a role, instead set User.account_type.

name.description:
Custom location name (e.g. building or site name like "London Office").
For cloud environments, this is the region (e.g. "us-west2").

deskName.description:
Desk name or individual location, typically for an employee in an
office.
(e.g. "IN-BLR-BCPC-11-1121D").

floorName.description:
Floor name, number or a combination of the two for a building.
(e.g. "1-A").

regionCoordinates.description:
Coordinates for the associated region.
See https://cloud.google.com/vision/docs/reference/rest/v1/LatLng
for a description of the fields.

Attribute.description:
Attribute is a container for generic entity attributes including common
attributes across core entities (such as, user or asset). For example, Cloud
is a generic entity attribute since it can apply to an asset (for example, a
VM) or a user (for example, an identity service account).

cloud.description:
Cloud metadata attributes such as project ID, account ID, or organizational
hierarchy.

labels.description:
Set of labels for the entity. Should only be used for product labels (for
example, Google Cloud resource labels or Azure AD sensitivity labels.
Should not be used for arbitrary key-value mappings.

permissions.description:
System permissions for IAM entity
(human principal, service account, group).

roles.description:
System IAM roles to be assumed by resources to use the role's permissions
for access control.

creationTime.description: Time the resource or entity was created or provisioned.

lastUpdateTime.description: Time the resource or entity was last updated.

Cloud.description: Metadata related to the cloud environment.

project.description:
The cloud environment project information.
Deprecated: Use Resource.resource_ancestors

availabilityZone.description:
The cloud environment availability zone (different from region which is
location.name).

Resource.description:
Information about a resource such as a task, Cloud Storage
bucket, database, disk, logical policy, or something similar.

type.description: Deprecated: use resource_type instead.

x-google-enum-descriptions: Endpoint that receive traffic from a load balancer or proxy.
Pod, which is a collection of containers. Often used in Kubernetes.
Credential, e.g. access keys, ssh keys, tokens, certificates.

id.description: Deprecated: Use resource.name or resource.product_object_id.

name.description:
The full name of the resource. For example,
Google Cloud: //cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com/projects/wombat-123,
and AWS: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/johndoe.

parent.description:
The parent of the resource.
For a database table, the parent is the database. For a storage object,
the bucket name.  Deprecated: use resource_ancestors.name.

productObjectId.description:
A vendor-specific identifier to uniquely identify the entity (a GUID,
OID, or similar)
This field can be used as an entity indicator for a Resource entity.

attribute.description: Generic entity metadata attributes of the resource.

scheduledTask.description:
DEPRECATED: use windows_scheduled_task for Windows scheduled tasks or
scheduled_cron_task for cron jobs.
Information about a scheduled task associated with the resource.

scheduledCronTask.description: Information about a scheduled cron task associated with the resource.

scheduledAnacronTask.description: Information about a scheduled anacron task associated with the resource.

windowsScheduledTask.description: Information about a Windows scheduled task associated with the resource.

volume.description: Information about a storage volume associated with the resource.

service.description: Information about a Windows service associated with the resource.

ScheduledTask.description:
Deprecated: use WindowsScheduledTask for Windows scheduled tasks or
ScheduledCronTask for cron jobs.
Information about a scheduled task.

weekDay.description: The day of the week (0-6, Sunday=0).

comment.description: A comment or description for the task.

author.description: The account name that authored or last modified the scheduled task.

ScheduledCronTask.description: Information about a scheduled cron task.

minute.description:
Crontab minute field. Value is an integer between 0 and 59 and can also be
a range or list of values (e.g., "0-59", "0-59/5", "0,15,30,45") and it  //
can also be an asterisk (*) to indicate first-last minutes. More on crontab
format can be found here: https://linux.die.net/man/5/crontab

hour.description:
Crontab hour field. Value is an integer between 0 and 23,
a range or list of values (e.g., "0-6", "*/2", "1,2"), or
an asterisk (*) to indicate first-last hours.

monthDay.description:
Crontab day of month field. Value is an integer between 1 and 31,
a range or list of values (e.g., "1-7", "1-31/7", "1,15"), or
an asterisk (*) to indicate first-last days of month.

month.description:
Crontab month field. Value is an integer between 1 and 12 or a 3-letter
name (e.g., "Jan"), a range or list of values (e.g.,
"1-3", "*/2", "1,6"), or an asterisk (*) to indicate first-last months.

weekDay.description:
Crontab day of week field. Value is an integer between 0 and 7 (0 or 7
is Sunday) or a 3-letter name (e.g., "Fri"), a range
or list of values (e.g., "1-5", "0,6"), or an asterisk
(*) to indicate first-last days of week.

comment.description: A comment or description for the task.

author.description: The author or creator of the task.

path.description: The PATH environment variable defined in the crontab file.

ScheduledAnacronTask.description: Information about a scheduled anacron task.

period.description:
Anacrontab period field. Value is an integer in days,
or a string like "@daily", "@weekly", or "@monthly".

delayMinutes.description: The delay in minutes before the job is run.

jobId.description: The unique identifier of the job.

path.description: The PATH environment variable defined in the anacrontab file.

sourceLine.description: The original source line from the anacrontab file.

WindowsScheduledTask.description: Information about a Windows scheduled task.

author.description: The account name that authored or last modified the scheduled task.

virtualPath.description: The task's path in the Task Scheduler library.

exitCode.description: The result which was returned the last time the registered task was run.

state.description: The operation state of the task.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The state of the task is unknown or not specified.

The task is registered but is disabled and no instances of the task are
queued or running. The task cannot be run until it is enabled.
Instances of the task are queued.
The task is ready to be executed, but no instances are queued or running.
One or more instances of the task are running.

logonType.description: The logon type of the task.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The logon method is not specified. Used for non-NT credentials.

Use a password for logging on the user. The password must be supplied at
registration time.

Use an existing interactive token to run a task. The user must log on
using a service for user (S4U) logon. When an S4U logon is used, no
password is stored by the system and there is no access to either the
network or encrypted files.

User must already be logged on. The task will be run only in an existing
interactive session.

Indicates that a Local System, Local Service, or Network Service account
is being used as a security context to run the task.

First use the interactive token. If the user is not logged on (no
interactive token is available), the password is used. The password
must be specified when a task is registered. This flag is not recommended
for new tasks because it is less reliable than TASK_LOGON_PASSWORD.

taskActions.description: The actions of the scheduled task.

taskTriggers.description: The triggers of the scheduled task.

actionType.description: The action type of the task.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The action type is not specified.

This action performs a command-line operation. For example, the action
can run a script, launch an executable, or, if the name of a document
is provided, find its associated application and launch the application
with the document.

This action fires a handler. This action can only be used if the task
Compatibility property is set to TASK_COMPATIBILITY_V2.

This action sends an email message. This action can only be used if the
task Compatibility property is set to TASK_COMPATIBILITY_V2.

This action shows a message box. This action can only be used if the
task Compatibility property is set to TASK_COMPATIBILITY_V2.

execArguments.description:
The arguments of the task. This field is only populated if the task
action type is EXEC.

execWorkingDirectory.description:
The executable working directory of the task. This field is only
populated if the task action type is EXEC.

comClassId.description:
The COM class IF the action is COM handler. This field is only populated
if the task action type is COM_HANDLER.

comData.description:
The data of the task. This field is only populated if the task action
type is COM_HANDLER.

TaskTrigger.description: The trigger of the scheduled task.

enabled.description: Indicates whether the task trigger is enabled.

duration.description: The duration of the task trigger repetition.

interval.description:
The interval between each repetition of the task. The format for this
string is PDTHMS (for example,
"PT5M" is 5 minutes, "PT1H" is 1 hour, and "PT20M" is 20 minutes).
The maximum time allowed is 31 days, and the minimum time allowed
is 1 minute.

triggerType.description: The trigger frequency of the task.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The trigger frequency is not specified.
Triggers the task when a specific event occurs.
Triggers the task at a specific time of day.

Triggers the task on a daily schedule. For example, the task starts at
a specific time every day, every other day, or every third day.

Triggers the task on a weekly schedule. For example, the task starts at
8:00 AM on a specific day every week or other week.

Triggers the task on a monthly schedule. For example, the task starts
on specific days of specific months.

Triggers the task on a monthly day-of-week schedule. For example, the
task starts on a specific days of the week, weeks of the month, and
months of the year.
Triggers the task when the computer goes into an idle state.
Triggers the task when the task is registered.
Triggers the task when the computer boots.
Triggers the task when a specific user logs on.
Triggers the task when a specific user session state changes.

fileSystem.description: The name of the file system on the volume (e.g., "NTFS", "FAT32").

mountPoint.description: The path where the volume is mounted (e.g., "C:", "/mnt/data").

devicePath.description: The system path to the device (e.g., "\\.\HarddiskVolume1", "/dev/sda1").

isMounted.description: Indicates whether the volume is currently mounted.

isReadOnly.description: Indicates whether the volume is mounted as read-only.

name.description: The user-assigned label or name for the volume.

displayName.description: The user-friendly display name of the service.

serviceType.description:
Deprecated: use service_types instead.
The type of service.
x-google-enum-descriptions:
A process that is owned by the service. This is a Windows-specific
service type.

A process that is shared by the service. This is a Windows-specific
service type.
An adapter. This is a Windows-specific service type.
A recognizer driver. This is a Windows-specific service type.
An interactive process. This is a Windows-specific service type.

A process that is owned by the service. This is a Windows-specific
service type.

A process that is shared by the service. This is a Windows-specific
service type.
An adapter. This is a Windows-specific service type.
A recognizer driver. This is a Windows-specific service type.
An interactive process. This is a Windows-specific service type.

startupType.description: The startup type of the service.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The service is started manually by a user.
The service is disabled and will not start automatically.
The service is stopped. This is a Windows-specific service status.
The service is paused. This is a Windows-specific service status.

source.description: Where the label is derived from.

rbacEnabled.description: Indicates whether this label can be used for Data RBAC

Permission.description: System permission for resource access and modification.

name.description: Name of the permission (e.g. chronicle.analyst.updateRule).

description.description: Description of the permission (e.g. 'Ability to update detect rules').

type.description: Type of the permission.

Role.description: System role for resource access and modification.

name.description: System role name for user.

description.description: System role description for user.

type.description: System role type for well known roles.
x-google-enum-descriptions: System service account for automated privilege access.

TimeOff.description:
System record for leave/time-off from a Human Capital Management (HCM)
system.

description.description: Description of the leave if available (e.g. 'Vacation').

Favicon.description: Difference hash and MD5 hash of the domain's favicon.

type.description: Type.

priority.description: Priority.

firstSeenTime.description: Date the certificate was first retrieved by VirusTotal.

signatureAlgorithm.description: Algorithm used for the signature (for example, "sha1RSA").

ca.description: Whether the subject acts as a certificate authority (CA) or not.

subjectKeyId.description: Identifies the public key being certified.

authorityKeyId.description:
Identifies the public key to be used to verify the signature on this
certificate or CRL.

keyUsage.description: The purpose for which the certified public key is used.

caInfoAccess.description:
Authority information access locations are URLs that are added to a
certificate in its authority information access extension.

crlDistributionPoints.description:
CRL distribution points to which a certificate user should refer to
ascertain if the certificate has been revoked.

extendedKeyUsage.description:
One or more purposes for which the certified public key may be used, in
addition to or in place of the basic purposes indicated in the key usage
extension field.

subjectAlternativeName.description:
Contains one or more alternative names, using any of a variety of name
forms, for the entity that is bound by the CA to the certified public
key.

certificatePolicies.description:
Different certificate policies will relate to different applications
which may use the certified key.

netscapeCertComment.description: Used to include free-form text comments inside certificates.

certTemplateNameDc.description: BMP data value "DomainController". See MS Q291010.

netscapeCertificate.description:
Identify whether the certificate subject is an SSL client, an SSL server,
or a CA.

peLogotype.description: Whether the certificate includes a logotype.

oldAuthorityKeyId.description: Whether the certificate has an old authority key identifier extension.

AuthorityKeyId.description:
Identifies the public key to be used to verify the signature on this
certificate or CRL.

algorithm.description:
Any of "RSA", "DSA" or "EC". Indicates the algorithm used to generate the
certificate.

PopularityRank.description:
Domain's position in popularity ranks for sources such as Alexa, Quantcast,
or Statvoo.

giver.description: Name of the rank serial number hexdump.

ingestionTime.description: Timestamp when the rank was ingested.

Artifact.description: Information about an artifact. The artifact can only be an IP.

ip.description:
IP address of the artifact.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for an external destination
IP entity.

prevalence.description: The prevalence of the artifact within the customer's environment.

firstSeenTime.description: First seen timestamp of the IP in the customer's environment.

lastSeenTime.description: Last seen timestamp of the IP address in the customer's environment.

location.description: Location of the Artifact's IP address.

network.description: Network information related to the Artifact's IP address.

asOwner.description: Owner of the Autonomous System to which the IP address belongs.

asn.description: Autonomous System Number to which the IP address belongs.

jarm.description:
The JARM hash for the IP address.
(https://engineering.salesforce.com/easily-identify-malicious-servers-on-the-internet-with-jarm-e095edac525a).

lastHttpsCertificate.description: SSL certificate information about the IP address.

lastHttpsCertificateDate.description: Most recent date for the certificate in VirusTotal.

regionalInternetRegistry.description: RIR (one of the current RIRs: AFRINIC, ARIN, APNIC, LACNIC or RIPE NCC).

whois.description: WHOIS information as returned from the pertinent WHOIS server.

whoisDate.description: Date of the last update of the WHOIS record in VirusTotal.

anonymous.description: Whether the VPN tunnels are configured for anonymous browsing or not.

artifactClient.description: Entity or software accessing or utilizing network resources.

risks.description: This field lists potential risks associated with the network activity.

sessionDuration.description:
The duration of the session as the number of seconds and nanoseconds.
For seconds, network.session_duration.seconds, the type is a 64-bit
integer. For nanoseconds, network.session_duration.nanos, the type is a
32-bit integer.

sessionId.description: The ID of the network session.

parentSessionId.description: The ID of the parent network session.

applicationProtocolVersion.description: The version of the application protocol. e.g. "1.1, 2.0"

x-google-enum-descriptions: Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine Protocol.
Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions and Secure MIME.

National Transportation Communications for Intelligent Transportation
System.
Styx/9P - Plan 9 from Bell Labs distributed file system protocol.

smtp.description:
SMTP info.
Store fields specific to SMTP not covered by Email.

isProxy.description: Whether the IP address is a known proxy.

proxyInfo.description: Proxy information. Only set if is_proxy is true.

connectionState.description: The state of the network connection.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The port is listening for incoming connections.
The connection is waiting for a timeout.

The connection is waiting for a connection termination request from the
local application.
A connection request has been sent.
A connection request has been received.

The connection is waiting for a connection termination request from the
remote host.

The connection is waiting for a connection termination request from the
local application.

The connection is waiting for an acknowledgment of the final connection
termination request.

subject.description: The subject line(s) of the email.

id.description: DNS query id.

response.description: Set to true if the event is a DNS response. See QR field from RFC1035.

opcode.description:
The DNS OpCode used to specify the type of DNS query
(for example, QUERY, IQUERY, or STATUS).

authoritative.description: Other DNS header flags. See RFC1035, section 4.1.1.

truncated.description: Whether the DNS response was truncated.

recursionDesired.description: Whether a recursive DNS lookup is desired.

recursionAvailable.description: Whether a recursive DNS lookup is available.

responseCode.description: Response code. See RCODE from RFC1035.

questions.description: A list of domain protocol message questions.

answers.description: A list of answers to the domain name query.

authority.description:
A list of domain name servers which verified the answers to the domain name
queries.

additional.description:
A list of additional domain name servers that can be used to verify the
answer to the domain.

name.description: The domain name.

type.description: The code specifying the type of the query.

class.description: The code specifying the class of the query.

prevalence.description: The prevalence of the domain within the customer's environment.

ResourceRecord.description: DNS Resource Records. See RFC1035, section 4.1.3.

name.description: The name of the owner of the resource record.

type.description: The code specifying the type of the resource record.

class.description: The code specifying the class of the resource record.

ttl.description:
The time interval for which the resource record can be cached before the
source of the information should again be queried.

data.description:
The payload or response to the DNS question for all responses encoded in
UTF-8 format

binaryData.description:
The raw bytes of any non-UTF8 strings that might be included as part of a
DNS response.

seconds.description: Seconds elapsed since client began address acquisition/renewal process.

siaddr.description: IP address of the next bootstrap server.

sname.description: Server name that the client wishes to boot from.

type.description: DHCP message type.

leaseTimeSeconds.description: Lease time in seconds. See RFC2132, section 9.2.

clientIdentifier.description:
Client identifier. See RFC2132, section 9.14.
Note: Make sure to update the client_identifier_string field as well if you
update this field.

requestedAddress.description: Requested IP address. See RFC2132, section 9.1.

clientIdentifierString.description:
Client identifier as string. See RFC2132, section 9.14.
This field holds the string value of the client_identifier.

Http.description:
Specify the full URL of the HTTP request within "target".
Also specify any uploaded or downloaded file information within "source"
or "target".

referralUrl.description: The URL for the HTTP referer.

userAgent.description:
The User-Agent request header which includes the application type,
operating system, software vendor or software version of the requesting
software user agent.

responseCode.description:
The response status code, for example
200, 302, 404, or 500.

family.description:
User agent family captures the type of browser/app at a high-level
 e.g. MSIE, Gecko, Safari etc..
x-google-enum-descriptions: Used to represent new families supported by user-defined parsers

Mobile and non-browser user agent families
UA's w/o enough data to fit into a family
Apple apps e.g. YouTube on iPhone
Windows Media Player, RSS platform etc...
Constructed from UA-CH instead of UserAgent string.

subFamily.description:
Sub-family identifies individual regexps when a family has more than 1.
This is used to generate the right UA string from a protobuf.
Examples in the AppleWebKit family: Chrome and Safari.
Can also be an arbitrary identifier.

platform.description:
The platform describes the environment in which the browser or app runs.
For desktop user agents, Platform is a string describing the OS family
 e.g. Windows, Macintosh, Linux.
For mobile user agents, Platform either describes the OS family (if
available) or the hardware maker.
 e.g. Linux, or HTC, LG, Palm.

device.description:
(Usually) Mobile specific: name of hardware device, may or may not
contain the full model name.
 e.g. iPhone, Palm750, SPH-M800.
Reduced to "K" for Android devices with reduced User-Agent and no client
hints (https://www.chromium.org/updates/ua-reduction/).

deviceVersion.description:
(Usually) Mobile specific: version of hardware device
Unavailable with reduced User-Agent and no client hints
(https://www.chromium.org/updates/ua-reduction/).

carrier.description: Mobile specific: name of mobile carrier

security.description:
Security level reported by user agent, either U, I or N.
Unavailable with reduced User-Agent and no client hints
(https://www.chromium.org/updates/ua-reduction/).

locale.description:
Locale in which the browser is running as country code and
optionally language pair.
Unavailable with reduced User-Agent and no client hints
(https://www.chromium.org/updates/ua-reduction/).

os.description:
Full name of the operating system
 e.g. "Darwin/9.7.0", "Android 1.5", "Windows 98"
Version is reduced, and other data might also be missing, for reduced
User-Agent and no client hints
(https://www.chromium.org/updates/ua-reduction/).

osVariant.description:
Extra qualifier for the OS
 e.g. "(i386)", "Build/CUPCAKE", "PalmSource/Palm-D061"
Unavailable with reduced User-Agent and no client hints
(https://www.chromium.org/updates/ua-reduction/).

browser.description:
Product brand within the family: Firefox, Netscape, Camino etc..
Or Earth, Windows-Media-Player etc.. for non-browser user agents.

browserVersion.description:
Minor and lower versions unavailable with reduced User-Agent and no client
hints (https://www.chromium.org/updates/ua-reduction/).

browserEngineVersion.description:
Version of the rendering engine
 e.g. "8.01" for "Opera/8.01"

googleToolbarVersion.description:
Version number of GoogleToolbar, if installed.
Applies only to MSIE and Firefox at this time.

Annotation.description:
Extra parameters that don't fit anywhere else, captured as key/value.
For example "VendorID/42" in BlackBerry user agents.

The following keys are modified with reduced User-Agent and no client hints
(https://www.chromium.org/updates/ua-reduction/):
*  "Chrome" (see `browser_version`)
*  "ChromiumBrowser" (unavailable)
*  "ChromeWebview" (unavailable)
*  "OS_VERSION" (see `os`)
*  "Rest" (unavailable)
*  "misc" (see `device`)

client.description: Certificate information for the client certificate.

server.description: Certificate information for the server certificate.

curve.description: Elliptical curve used for a given cipher.

established.description: Indicates whether the TLS negotiation was successful.

nextProtocol.description: Protocol to be used for tunnel.

resumed.description:
Indicates whether the TLS connection was resumed from a previous
TLS negotiation.

Client.description:
Transport Layer Security (TLS) information associated with the client
(for example, Certificate or JA3 hash).

ja3.description: JA3 hash from the TLS ClientHello, as a hex-encoded string.

serverName.description: Host name of the server, that the client is connecting to.

supportedCiphers.description: Ciphers supported by the client during client hello.

md5.description: The MD5 hash of the certificate, as a hex-encoded string.

sha1.description: The SHA1 hash of the certificate, as a hex-encoded string.

sha256.description: The SHA256 hash of the certificate, as a hex-encoded string.

notBefore.description: Indicates when the certificate is first valid.

notAfter.description: Indicates when the certificate is no longer valid.

Server.description:
Transport Layer Security (TLS) information associated with the server
(for example, Certificate or JA3 hash).

ja3s.description: JA3 hash from the TLS ServerHello, as a hex-encoded string.

serverResponse.description: The server's response(s) to the client.

messagePath.description: The message's path (extracted from the headers).

isWebmail.description: If the message was sent via a webmail client.

isTls.description: If the connection switched to TLS.

anonymous.description: Whether the IP address is anonymous.

anonymousVpn.description: Whether the IP address is an anonymous VPN.

publicProxy.description: Whether the IP address is a public proxy.

torExitNode.description: Whether the IP address is a tor exit node.

smartDnsProxy.description: Whether the IP address is a smart DNS proxy.

hostingProvider.description: Whether the IP address is a hosting provider.

vpnDatacenter.description: Whether the IP address is a VPN datacenter.

residentialProxy.description: Whether the IP address is a residential proxy.

vpnServiceName.description: The name of the VPN service.

proxyOverVpn.description: Whether the IP address is a proxy over VPN.

relayProxy.description: Whether the IP address is a relay proxy.

provider.description: The provider of the VPN tunnels being used.

type.description: The type of the VPN tunnels.

ArtifactClient.description: Entity or software accessing or utilizing network resources.

behaviors.description: The behaviors of the client accessing the network.

proxies.description: The type of proxies used by the client.

favicon.description: Difference hash and MD5 hash of the URL's.

htmlMeta.description: Meta tags (only for URLs downloading HTML).

lastFinalUrl.description: If the original URL redirects, where does it end.

lastHttpResponseCode.description: HTTP response code of the last response.

lastHttpResponseContentLength.description: Length in bytes of the content received.

lastHttpResponseHeaders.description: Headers and values of the last HTTP response.

trackers.description: Trackers found in the URL in a historical manner.

id.description: Tracker ID, if available.

Browser.description: Information about an entry in the web browser's local history database.

browserType.description:
The browser that recorded the history entry (e.g. "Chrome", "Firefox",
"Safari", etc.).

firstVisitTime.description: The timestamp indicating the initial visit to the URL.

lastVisitTime.description: The timestamp indicating the most recent visit to the URL.

profile.description: The browser profile associated with the history entry.

typed.description: A boolean value indicating if the URL was typed by the user.

visitType.description: Describes the type of navigation or visit (e.g., direct, redirect, etc.).
x-google-enum-descriptions: Loaded in a nested subframe by the parent frame.
Loaded in a nested subframe by the user.
The user clicked on auto generated link in browser address bar.
The page was loaded through command line or is the starting page.
The Url was generated by a keyword search configured by user.
Corresponds to a visit generated by a keyword search.
The user was redirected to the URL.

hidden.description: A boolean value indicating if the history entry is hidden.

requestOriginUri.description: Indicates the URI from which the current visit originated.

visitCount.description: The total number of times the Url has been visited.

visitCountCriteria.description: Describes the criteria used to calculate the visit_count.

indexedContent.description:
Represents the textual content of a web page. This field should be kept
short. Large strings may affect latency and payload sizes.

firstBookmarkedTime.description: The timestamp indicating the first time the URL was bookmarked.

typedCount.description:
The number of times the URL was visited with this specific visit type and
visit source.

x-google-enum-descriptions: The visit was synced from another device.
The visit was from a browser.
The visit was from an extension.
The visit was imported from another browser application.

name.description: The unique name identifying the cookie.

value.description: The data stored within the cookie.

domain.description: The domain for which the cookie is valid.

path.description: The URL path for which the cookie is valid.

expirationTime.description: The date and time when the cookie will expire.

httpOnly.description:
Indicates if the cookie is inaccessible via client-side scripts (e.g.,
JavaScript).

secure.description:
Indicates if the cookie should only be sent over secure HTTPS
connections.

maxAge.description: The maximum age of the cookie in seconds.

session.description: Indicates if the cookie is persistent.

partitioned.description: Shows if the cookies is stored using partitioned storage.

productObjectId.description:
Product globally unique user object identifier, such as an LDAP Object
Identifier.

creationTime.description:
Group creation time.
Deprecated: creation_time should be populated in Attribute as generic
metadata.

attribute.description: Generic entity metadata attributes of the group.

windowsSid.description: Microsoft Windows SID of the group.

pid.description:
The process ID.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for process entities.

parentPid.description:
The ID of the parent process.
Deprecated: use parent_process.pid instead.

file.description: Information about the file in use by the process.

commandLine.description:
The command line command that created the process.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for process entities.

commandLineHistory.description: The command line history of the process.

accessMask.description: A bit mask representing the level of access.

integrityLevelRid.description: The Microsoft Windows integrity level relative ID (RID) of the process.

euid.description: The effective user ID of the process.

ruid.description: The real user ID of the process.

egid.description: The effective group ID of the process.

rgid.description: The real group ID of the process.

pgid.description: The identifier that points to the process group ID leader.

sessionLeaderPid.description: The process ID of the session leader process.

tty.description: The teletype terminal which the command was executed within.

tokenElevationType.description:
The elevation type of the process on Microsoft Windows. This determines if
any privileges are removed when UAC is enabled.
x-google-enum-descriptions: A full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.

An elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled. Used
when running as administrator.

A limited token with administrative privileges removed and
administrative groups disabled.

productSpecificParentProcessId.description:
A product specific id for the parent process.
Please use parent_process.product_specific_process_id instead.

ipv6.description: This is used to determine if the process is an IPv6 process.

kernelDuration.description: The kernel time spent in the process.

userDuration.description: The user time spent in the process.

realDuration.description:
The real time spent in the process. This is the sum of the kernel and user
time.

x-google-enum-descriptions: Process is waiting for an event.
Process is in uninterruptible sleep, typically I/O.
Process is terminated but not reaped by parent.
Process is woken to be killed.
Linux, macOS, and FreeBSD specific: process is idle.

sha256.description:
The SHA256 hash of the file, as a hex-encoded string.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for file entities.

md5.description:
The MD5 hash of the file, as a hex-encoded string.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for file entities.

sha1.description:
The SHA1 hash of the file, as a hex-encoded string.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for file entities.

size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

fullPath.description:
The full path identifying the location of the file on the system.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for file entities.

mimeType.description:
The MIME (Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions) type of the file,
for example "PE", "PDF", or "powershell script".

fileMetadata.description:
Metadata associated with the file.
Deprecate FileMetadata in favor of using fields in File.

securityResult.description:
Google Cloud Threat Intelligence (GCTI) security result for the file
including threat context and detection metadata.

peFile.description: Metadata about the Portable Executable (PE) file.

symhash.description:
SymHash of the file. Used for Mach-O (e.g. MacOS) binaries, to identify
similar files based on their symbol table.

x-google-enum-descriptions:
Although DLLs are actually portable executables, this value
enables the file type to be identified separately.
File type is PE_DLL.

File type is PSD.
Adobe Photoshop.

File type is RM.
RealMedia type.
File type is MSIX, new Windows app package format.

File type is BAT, Windows .bat/.cmd (old files are tagged as
SHELLSCRIPT).
File type is MSC, Microsoft Management Console (MMC).
File type is RDP, Microsoft Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) file.

lastModificationTime.description: Timestamp when the file was last updated.

createTime.description: Timestamp when the file was created.

lastAccessTime.description: Timestamp when the file was accessed.

prevalence.description: Prevalence of the file hash in the customer's environment.

firstSeenTime.description: Timestamp the file was first seen in the customer's environment.

lastSeenTime.description: Timestamp the file was last seen in the customer's environment.

statMode.description:
The mode of the file. A bit string indicating the permissions and
privileges of the file.

statInode.description: The file identifier. Unique identifier of object within a file system.

statDev.description: The file system identifier to which the object belongs.

lastAnalysisTime.description: Timestamp the file was last analysed.

embeddedUrls.description: Embedded urls found in the file.

embeddedDomains.description: Embedded domains found in the file.

embeddedIps.description: Embedded IP addresses found in the file.

exifInfo.description: Exif metadata from different file formats extracted by exiftool.

signatureInfo.description: File signature information extracted from different tools.

pdfInfo.description: Information about the PDF file structure.

firstSubmissionTime.description: First submission time of the file.

lastSubmissionTime.description: Last submission time of the file.

FileMetadata.description:
Metadata about a file.
Place metadata about different file types here, for example data
from the Microsoft Windows VersionInfo block or digital signer details.
Use a different sub-message per file type.

pe.description:
Metadata for Microsoft Windows PE files.
Deprecate PeFileMetadata in favor of single File proto.

PeFileMetadata.description: Metadata about a Microsoft Windows Portable Executable.

FileMetadataPE.description: Metadata about the Portable Executable (PE) file.

resourcesTypeCountStr.description:
Number of resources by resource type.
Example: RT_ICON: 10, RT_DIALOG: 5

resourcesLanguageCountStr.description:
Number of resources by language.
Example: NEUTRAL: 20, ENGLISH US: 10

signatureInfo.description:
FilemetadataSignatureInfo field.
deprecated, user File.signature_info instead.

name.description: Name of the section.

filetypeMagic.description: Type of resource content, as identified by the magic Python module.

languageCode.description:
Human-readable version of the language and sublanguage identifiers, as
defined in the Microsoft Windows PE specification.
Examples:
| Language     | Sublanguage        | Field value |
| LANG_NEUTRAL | SUBLANG_NEUTRAL    | NEUTRAL     |
| LANG_FRENCH  | -                  | FRENCH      |
| LANG_ENGLISH | SUBLANG_ENGLISH US | ENGLISH US  |

fileType.description:
File type.
Note that this value may not match any of the well-known type identifiers
defined in the ResourceType enum.

verificationMessage.description:
Status of the certificate.
Valid values are "Signed", "Unsigned" or a description of the certificate
anomaly, if found.

signers.description:
File metadata signer information.
The order of the signers matters. Each element is a higher level
authority, being the last the root authority.

SignerInfo.description: File metadata related to the signer information.

name.description:
Common name of the signers/certificate.
The order of the signers matters. Each element is a higher level
authority, the last being the root authority.

status.description:
It can say "Valid" or state the problem with the certificate if any (e.g.
"This certificate or one of the certificates in the certificate chain is
not time valid.").

validUsage.description:
Indicates which situations the certificate is valid for (e.g. "Code
Signing").

name.description: Certificate name.

runCount.description: The number of times the application has been run.

prefetchHash.description: A hash of the executable path used to identify the prefetch file.

SignatureInfo.description: File signature information extracted from different tools.

sigcheck.description: Signature information extracted from the sigcheck tool.

codesign.description: Signature information extracted from the codesign utility.

FileMetadataCodesign.description: File metadata from the codesign utility.

id.description: Code sign identifier.

teamId.description: The assigned team identifier of the developer who signed the application.

PDFInfo.description: Information about the PDF file structure. Seehttps://developers.virustotal.com/reference/pdf_info

js.description:
Number of /JS tags found in the PDF file. Should be the same as
javascript field in normal scenarios.

javascript.description:
Number of /JavaScript tags found in the PDF file. Should be the same as
the js field in normal scenarios.

launchActionCount.description: Number of /Launch tags found in the PDF file.

acroform.description: Number of /AcroForm tags found in the PDF.

autoaction.description: Number of /AA tags found in the PDF.

embeddedFile.description: Number of /EmbeddedFile tags found in the PDF.

encrypted.description:
Whether the document is encrypted or not. This is defined by the /Encrypt
tag.

flash.description: Number of /RichMedia tags found in the PDF.

jbig2Compression.description: Number of /JBIG2Decode tags found in the PDF.

endstreamCount.description: Number of defined stream objects (stream keyword).

pageCount.description: Number of pages in the PDF.

streamCount.description: Number of defined stream objects (stream keyword).

openaction.description: Number of /OpenAction tags found in the PDF.

startxref.description: Number of startxref keywords in the PDF.

suspiciousColors.description: Number of colors expressed with more than 3 bytes (CVE-2009-3459).

trailer.description: Number of trailer keywords in the PDF.

xfa.description: Number of \XFA tags found in the PDF.

xref.description: Number of xref keywords in the PDF.

allocated.description: Indicates whether the file is allocated in the Master File Table (MFT).

reason.description:
Deprecated: Use reasons instead.
Human-readable string describing the reason for the USN journal entry.
(e.g., "USN_REASON_FILE_CREATE").

reasons.description:
Human-readable string describing the reasons for the USN journal entry
(e.g., "USN_REASON_FILE_CREATE").

sequence.description:
Indicates the chronological order in which the entry was added to the
cache.

executed.description: Indicates whether the file associated with the entry was executed.

controlSet.description:
Indicates which registry Control Set the AppCompatCache data belongs to
(e.g., "ControlSet001").

Asset.description:
Information about a compute asset such as a workstation, laptop, phone,
virtual desktop, or VM.

productObjectId.description:
A vendor-specific identifier to uniquely identify the entity (a GUID  or
similar).
This field can be used as an entity indicator for asset entities.

hostname.description:
Asset hostname or domain name field.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for asset entities.

assetId.description:
The asset ID. Value must contain the ':' character. For example,
cs:abcdd23434.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for asset entities.

ip.description:
A list of IP addresses associated with an asset.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for asset entities.

mac.description:
List of MAC addresses associated with an asset.
This field can be used as an entity indicator for asset entities.

natIp.description: List of NAT IP addresses associated with an asset.

firstSeenTime.description:
The first observed time for an asset.
The value is calculated on the basis of the
first time the identifier was observed.

platformSoftware.description: The asset operating system platform software.

category.description: The category of the asset (e.g. "End User Asset", "Workstation", "Server").

type.description: The type of the asset (e.g. workstation or laptop or server).
x-google-enum-descriptions: A mobile device such as a mobile phone or PDA.

networkDomain.description: The network domain of the asset (e.g. "corp.acme.com")

creationTime.description:
Time the asset was created or provisioned.
Deprecate: creation_time should be populated in Attribute as generic
metadata.

firstDiscoverTime.description:
Time the asset was first discovered (by asset management/discoverability
software).

lastDiscoverTime.description:
Time the asset was last discovered (by asset management/discoverability
software).

systemLastUpdateTime.description:
Time the asset system or OS was last updated.
For all other operations that are not system updates (such as resizing a
VM), use Attribute.last_update_time.

lastBootTime.description: Time the asset was last boot started.

labels.description:
Metadata labels for the asset.
Deprecated: labels should be populated in Attribute as generic metadata.

deploymentStatus.description: The deployment status of the asset for device lifecycle purposes.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Asset is active, functional and deployed.
Asset is pending decommission and no longer deployed.

attribute.description: Generic entity metadata attributes of the asset.

wmiPersistenceItem.description: Information about a WMI persistence item.

Hardware.description:
Hardware specification details for a resource, including both physical and
virtual hardware.

cpuPlatform.description: Platform of the hardware CPU (e.g. "Intel Broadwell").

cpuModel.description:
Model description of the hardware CPU
(e.g. "2.8 GHz Quad-Core Intel Core i5").

watchlists.description: Optional. The watchlists from the specified instance.
