Optional. A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

ListThreatCollectionsResponse.description: Response containing a list of threat collections.

threatCollections.description:
Output only. List of threat collections. This is sorted by creation time in descending
order by default.

nextPageToken.description:
Output only. A token that can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

ThreatCollection.description:
A threat collection from Google Threat Intelligence which represents either a
threat campaign or a threat report.

name.description: Output only. Identifier. The resource name of this ThreatCollection.

displayName.description: Output only. Display name of the threat collection.

description.description:
Output only. Description of the threat collection.
For campaigns, this is the description/context about the campaign.
For reports, this is the ML-autogenerated summary of the report's content,
generated by GTI.

threatCollectionType.description: Output only. Type of the threat collection.

createTime.description: Output only. Time when the threat collection was created.

updateTime.description: Output only. Time when the threat collection was last modified.

altNames.description:
Output only. Alternative display names of the threat collection.
For example, 'CAMP.22.007' for an individual campaign threat collection.

targetedIndustries.description: Output only. The industries that are targeted by the threat collection.

targetedRegions.description:
Output only. The regions that are targeted by the threat collection, represented as
ISO-3166-2 country codes.

content.description: Output only. The full Report content. Only available for ThreatCollectionType.REPORT.

executiveSummary.description:
Output only. The summary of the content of the report. Only available for
ThreatCollectionType.REPORT.

FetchIocMatchMetadataResponse.description: Response containing a list of ioc match metadata.

iocMatchMetadata.description: Output only. The list of ioc match metadata requested.

IocMatchMetadata.description: IOC match metadata for a threat collection.

threatCollection.description:
The GTI ID of the ThreatCollection that has IOC matches
(e.g. "campaign--604aff2a-754f-5b8d-8d37-c00711ffab43").

iocMatchesCount.description: Number of ioc matches that correlates to the threat_collection

FetchThreatCollectionEntityMetadataRespo.description: Response containing a list of entity metadata.

entityMetadata.description:
Output only. A list of entity risks scores with information typing them to the threat
collection. Ordered by entity.risk_score.

nextPageToken.description:
Output only. A token that can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description:
Output only. The total number of entities that match the request.
The size of the `entity_metadata` returned by pagination may be less than
the `total_size` that matches.

ThreatCollectionEntityMetadata.description: Entity metadata for a threat collection.

entity.description:
An Asset or User entity which contains an entity indicator and risk_score
data associated with it.

references.description: A list of references associated with the entity.

ThreatCollectionEntityMetadataReference.description:
A link between a threat collection and an Asset or User entity in a threat
collection's entity metadata.

ioc.description:
The resource name of the Ioc associated with the entity. Format:
projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/iocs/{ioc}

FetchRelatedThreatCollectionsResponse.description: Response containing a list of threat collections for a given threat artifact.

threatCollections.description: Output only. The list of ThreatCollections.

nextPageToken.description:
Output only. A token that can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description:
Output only. The total number of ThreatCollections that match the request.
The count of `threat_collections` returned by pagination may be less than
the `total_size` that matches.

ThreatCollectionFilterSet.description:
Represents threat collection filters and their available values to be used
when requesting a filtered list of threat collections.

name.description:
Output only. Identifier. Resource name for threat collection filters, in the format
'.../instances/{instance}/threatCollectionFilterSet'.

filters.description: Output only. List of filters that threat collections feed supports.

Filter.description:
Represents a threat collection filter and its available values to be used
when requesting a filtered list of threat collections.

displayName.description: Output only. Display name of the filter. Example: "Source Regions".

options.description: Output only. List of options under this filter.

FilterOption.description:
Threat collection filter option.
Example: 'filter=(source_region:"US")'.
"source_region" is the filter_key.
"US" is the value.

displayName.description: Output only. Display name of the filter option. Example: "United States".

filterKey.description:
Output only. The filtering key used to use when requesting a filtered list of threat
collections. Example: "source_region".

value.description:
Output only. Value of the filter to use when requesting a filtered list of threat
collections. Example: "US".

options.description: Output only. Nested list of options for a given filter option.

CaseQueueFilter.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
Case Queue Filters enable users to narrow your case search in the queue to
precisely target the cases you want to analyze, by saving and editing filters
that are significantly useful for them.

displayName.description:
Required. This is the name of the case queue filter that will be applied to the case
filter.

filterOperator.description:
Required. Specifies the logical operator for combining multiple filtering criteria.
Required if Criteria is available.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Default value. This value is unused.
The value is equal to any of the values.
The value is equal to all of the values.

criteria.description: Optional. List of filters to apply, optional.

timeRangeFilter.description: Optional. Specifies the time range filter type to apply.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Search for cases for the last day.
Search for cases for the last 2 days.
Search for cases for the last 3 days.
Search for cases for the last 7 days.
Search for cases for the last year.

startTime.description: Optional. Specifies the start time for filtering cases based on timestamp.

endTime.description: Optional. Specifies the end time for filtering cases based on timestamp.

author.description: Output only. Specifies the author of the case queue filter.

CaseQueueCriteria.description: The filtering criteria that each case queue filter can have.

type.description: Required. The type of the filter.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Default value. This value is unused.
The value is Environments dynamic parameters.
The value is Data Access Scopes.

operator.description: Required. The comparison operator used for the filter.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Default value. This value is unused.
The value is equal to any of the selected values.
The value is not equal to any of the selected values.

values.description: Required. The values of the filter.

includeUsers.description:
Optional. If the filter is of type Analysts (Roles / users) , and this is set to
True, then the BE will also search for cases assigned to users that are a
part of the roles in the search. "Include users associated with selected
roles" in the UI. Otherwise the related to the selected roles users will
not be included in the result.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description: The total number of CaseQueueFilters.

CaseStageDefinition.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
CaseStageDefinitions represents a stage in the lifecycle of a case.

displayName.description:
Required. Immutable. The display name of the new stage to create.
The display name must be unique within the instance.
The display name must not contain special characters.

order.description: Required. Immutable. Defines the case stage order in the lifetime of a case.

caseStageDefinitions.description:
The list of CaseStageDefinitions.
Ordered by Id by default.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description:
The total number of CaseStageDefinitions includes filter without
pagination.

CaseTagDefinition.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
Tags are assigned to cases by Google Security Operations based on predefined
rules and can be used to classify cases or find specific cases faster. Note
that you can manually add tags to a case from the Cases page. These tags can
be removed from the case, but NOT removed entirely from the system.

displayName.description: Required. This is the name of the tag that will be applied to the case.

matchCriteria.description: Required. Classify cases based on a criteria.
x-google-enum-descriptions: e.g. by one of the unique entities.

value.description: Required. Specific value to search in case - in addition to SearchIn property.

propertyName.description:
Optional. Specific Entity property name to search in case.
this is relevant only when a SearchIn of type BY_ENTITY_PROPERTY_NAME  was
chosen.

comparisonType.description:
Required. The type of comparison to be used when comparing the value to the case.
This is relevant only when a SearchIn of type BY_ENTITY_PROPERTY_NAME was
chosen.

priority.description:
Required. Note that Google Security Operations merges priority with other alerts and
entities and events so that the priority here is not absolute.

canBeCaseTitle.description:
Required. When checked, the tag will be assigned as the title of the case if it meets
the conditions. default is false.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description: The total number of CaseTagDefinitions.

content.description: Required. The CSV file content containing CaseTagDefinitions to import.

overrideExistingRecords.description: Optional. Whether to override existing CaseTagDefinition records during import.

CaseWallRecord.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
Case Wall is a repository of all event logs containing "case caseWallRecords"
related to a case since the time the case was created until it is closed,
taken by a user or a playbook's action.

caseId.description: Output only. The ID of the case associated with the activity.

creatorUserId.description:
Output only. The ID of the user who created the activity, e.g.
C7A739D7-E465-4106-9871-18F415D6C3BD

creatorFullName.description: Output only. The full name of the user who created the activity.

activityId.description: Output only. Id of the Case Wall activity. Replaces old prop: "Id"

activityKind.description: Optional. The kind of the activity.

favorite.description: Optional. Whether the activity is was marked as favorite.

createTime.description: Output only. The creation time of the activity in milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

updateTime.description: Output only. The modification time of the activity in milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

alertIdentifier.description:
Output only. The identifier of the alert (if any) associated with the activity.
for example, "SUSPICIOUS PHISHING
EMAIL_C7A739D7-E465-4106-9871-18F415D6C3BD"

activityDataJson.description:
Output only. Case activity details. Can be customized.
e.g. "Message": "Closing case"

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description: Total number of CaseWallRecords.

recordId.description: Required. The ID of the wall record to set as a favorite.

activityType.description: Required. The type of the activity.

activityKind.description: Optional. The kind of the activity.

favorite.description: Required. Whether the activity is was marked as favorite.

FetchActivitiesCountResponse.description:
Response message for FetchActivitiesCount this return the number of
activities for a given type.

actions.description: Output only. The number of actions type wall records.

statusChanges.description: Output only. The number of status changes type wall records.

tasks.description: Output only. The number of tasks type wall records.

comments.description: Output only. The number of comments type wall records.

insights.description: Output only. The number of insights type wall records.

pinnedChatMessages.description: Output only. The number of pinned chat messages type wall records.

favorite.description: Output only. The number of favorite type wall records.

operations.description: A list of operations that matches the specified filter in the request.

nextPageToken.description: The standard List next-page token.

unreachable.description:
Unordered list. Unreachable resources. Populated when the request sets
`ListOperationsRequest.return_partial_success` and reads across
collections. For example, when attempting to list all resources across all
supported locations.

Status.description:
The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for
different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is
used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains
three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details.

You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the
[API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

message.description:
A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any
user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the
google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

details.description:
A list of messages that carry the error details.  There is a common set of
message types for APIs to use.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

ConnectorEvent.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
ConnectorEvent - Chronicle Connector Event.
Types of ConnectorEvents: general, case-spesific

createTime.description: Output only. The create_time of the ConnectorEvent.

updateTime.description: Output only. The update_time of the ConnectorEvent.

alertIdentifier.description: Output only. The alert_identifier of the ConnectorEvent.

environment.description: Output only. The environment of the ConnectorEvent.

id.description: Output only. The id of the ConnectorEvent.

Ordered by Id by default.

fields.description: Output only. The fields of the event.

identifier.description: Output only. The identifier of the event.

name.description: Output only. The name of the event.

product.description: Output only. The product of the event.

port.description: Output only. The port of the event.

artifactEntities.description: Output only. The artifact entities of the event.

order.description: Output only. The order of the group.

groupName.description: Output only. The name of the group.

isIntegration.description: Output only. Indicates if the group is an integration.

isHighlight.description: Output only. Indicates if the group is a highlight.

items.description: Output only. The items in the group.

originalName.description: Output only. The original name of the property.

name.description: Output only. The name of the property.

value.description: Output only. Value of the property.

ConnectorLog.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
Describes a connector's log.

time.description:
Output only. Connector execution start time.
Start time is UTC time.

logLevel.description:
Output only. Connector execution end time.
Start time is UTC time.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description: Output only. Total number of ConnectorInstanceLogs.

ConnectorInstance.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
This resource describes the connector instances found on the Connectors
Management page..

updateAvailable.description: Output only. Indicates if the connector instance has an available update.

custom.description:
Output only. Determines whether this connector instance is based on a custom connector
definition.

enabled.description: Optional. Determines whether this connector is enabled or not.

remote.description:
Output only. Determines whether this connector instance is executed over a remote agent.
This cannot be patched and must be defined when creating the instance.
It is based on the agentIdentifier's value, if the identifier is null then
the      // connector is local.

agent.description:
Optional. For a connector that runs on an agent, the agent identifier.
This cannot be patched and must be defined when creating the instance

environment.description:
Required. Connector instance environment.
In case of a remote connector this cannot be patched

displayName.description: Required. Connector instance display name.

description.description: Optional. Connector instance description.

intervalSeconds.description: Required. Connector instance execution interval in seconds.

documentationLink.description: Output only. The documentation link to show in the UI.

timeoutSeconds.description: Required. Set the timeout of a single Python script run.

integrationVersion.description: Optional. The integration version. Required on patch.

version.description: Optional. Determines the connector instance version.

loggingEnabledUntilUnixMs.description: Optional. Determines the timeout when log collecting will be disabled.

parameters.description: Optional. Connector instance parameters.

connectorId.description: Output only. The connector definition's ID, or null if it doesn't exist.

identifier.description: Output only. The connector instance identifier.

id.description: Optional. The connector instance id.

pastDayAlertsCount.description: Output only. The connector instance ingested alerts over the last 24 hours.

avgAlertsPerDay.description: Output only. The average number of the daily ingested alerts by the connector instance.

advanced.description: Output only. Describes if the parameter is an advanced parameter.

value.description: Optional. The value of the parameter.

displayName.description: Output only. The parameter's display name.

type.description: Output only. The parameter's type.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Values as semi-colon separated string parameter type.

description.description: Output only. The parameter's description.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description: Output only. Total number of connector instances.

RunOnDemandConnectorInstanceRequest.description: Initiate a single execution of the connector, usually for testing purposes.

connectorInstance.description: Required. The connector instance with values from FE.

debugOutput.description: Output only. The execution debug output message

success.description: Output only. True if the execution was successful, false if not.

sampleCases.description:
Output only. The alerts that were produced by the connector run.
Could also be an empty list when no alerts were produced by the connector.

displayName.description: Output only. The display name of the sample case

productName.description: Output only. The product name of the alert

startTime.description: Output only. The start time of the sample case

endTime.description: Output only. The end time of the sample case

eventCount.description: Output only. The number of events in the alert

fullCaseInfoAsJson.description: Output only. The sample case full info represented as json

SetLogsCollectionRequest.description: Enables / disables the logs collection for a connector instance.

enabled.description: Required. Whether logs collection is enabled for the connector instance.

logEnableExpirationTime.description: Output only. The connector instance logging enabled time until unix ms

IntegrationConnector.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
This resource describes the connector's definition.

displayName.description: Required. Connector's definition name.

author.description: Output only. User that created the connector in the system.

description.description: Optional. Connector's description.

enabled.description: Required. Determines whether the connector is disabled or enabled.

custom.description: Output only. Determines if the connector is a custom or commercial connector.

productFieldName.description: Required. The field name used to determine the device product.

eventFieldName.description: Required. The field name used to determine the event name (sub-type).

timeoutSeconds.description: Required. Set the timeout of a single Python script run.

parameters.description: Optional. The connector's parameters definition.

id.description: Output only. The parameter's id.

mandatory.description:
Required. Indicates whether this parameter is mandatory for configuring a
connector instance.

defaultValue.description:
Optional. The default value of the parameter.
The default_value is required for booleans / mandatory parameters.

displayName.description: Required. The parameter's display name.

type.description: Required. The parameter's type.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Values as semicolon separated string parameter type.

description.description: Optional. The parameter's description.

ConnectorRule.description: Connector rules are used for allow/block lists rules.

id.description: Output only. The parameter's id.

displayName.description: Required. Connector's rule data name.

type.description: Required. Connector's rule data type.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description: Total number of Integration Connectors.

template.description: Output only. The connector definition template.

agentIdentifier.description: Optional. The agent identifier for remote testing

connector.description:
Required. The connector object to test
The full connector object is needed as there is an option to run the test
without saving the connector first.

outputMessage.description: Output only. Human-readable output message set by the script.

debugOutputMessage.description: Output only. The script debug output.

resultJson.description: Output only. The result json if exists.

ListConnectorRevisionsResponse.description:
ListConnectorRevisionsResponse contains the list of the specific connector's
ConnectorRevisions.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description: Output only. The total number of ConnectorRevisions in the instance.

ConnectorRevision.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
Allows saving & restoring different revisions of connector definitions.

snapshot.description: Output only. The snapshot of the connector.

createTime.description: Output only. Specifies the time the revision was created.

comment.description: Optional. Comment describing the revision, limited to 400 characters.

connector.description: Required. Used for creating a new revision.

author.description: Output only. The SOAR USER who created the specific revision.

RollbackConnectorRevisionRequest.description:
RollbackConnectorRevisionRequest rolls back the connector definition to a
saved revision.

ContentPack.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
ContentPack - Chronicle SOAR  Content Pack.

title.description: Output only. The title of the content pack, limited to 200 characters.

custom.description: Output only. Determines if the content-pack is custom or commercial.

highlighted.description: Output only. Represent if the content pack is marked as highlighted. Used by a client.

categories.description:
Output only. Categories used for filtering. Each category name is limited to 50
characters.

description.description:
Output only. Provides a detailed description of the content-pack, suitable for display
in a list view. Limited to 200 characters.

videoUri.description:
Output only. URL of e video associated with this content pack.
Must be a valid URL with a maximum length of 2048 characters.

uploader.description:
Output only. The uploader of the content pack, might not be a registered user of the
platform.

ontology.description: Output only. Represents if Content Pack has ontology

conflicts.description: Output only. Represents if Content Pack has ontology conflicts

integrations.description: Output only. The integrations of the content pack

connectorDefinitions.description: Output only. The connectors definitions of the content pack

connectorInstances.description: Output only. The connectors instances of the content pack

testCases.description: Output only. The test cases of the content pack

playbooks.description: Output only. The playbooks of the content pack

detectionRules.description: Output only. The detection rules of the content pack

searchQueries.description: Output only. The search query records of the content pack

deployed.description: Output only. Represents if Content Pack is deployed

dashboards.description: Output only. The dashboards records of the content pack

preInstallationGuidance.description: Output only. a guidance for the customer before installing the content pack.

postInstallationGuidance.description: Output only. a guidance for the customer after installing the content pack.

type.description: Output only. Enum type of content pack

identifier.description: Output only. The identifier of the content pack.

community.description: Output only. Represents if Content Pack is available in community

image.description: Output only. The image of the content pack.

ruleSets.description: Output only. The rule sets of the content pack

sourceType.description: Output only. The source type of the content pack.

externalLinkUrl.description: Output only. The external link of the content pack.

ContentPackIntegrationItem - Represents an integration item of a content
pack.

title.description: Output only. The display name of the item

custom.description: Output only. Represent if the content item is custom or commercial

iconSvg.description: Output only. SVG icon for representation, limited to 1MB.

ontology.description: Output only. Represents if integration has ontology

conflicts.description: Output only. Represents if integration ontology has conflicts

version.description: Output only. Represents the integration version

id.description: Output only. The unique ID of the content pack item.

title.description: Output only. The display name of the item

custom.description: Output only. Represent if the content item is custom or commercial

testCases.description:

playbooks.description:

Date.description:
Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday. The time of
day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The
date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the
following:

* A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values.
* A month and day, with a zero year (for example, an anniversary).
* A year on its own, with a zero month and a zero day.
* A year and month, with a zero day (for example, a credit card expiration
  date).

Related types:

* google.type.TimeOfDay
* google.type.DateTime
* google.protobuf.Timestamp

year.description:
Year of the date. Must be from 1 to 9999, or 0 to specify a date without
a year.

month.description:
Month of a year. Must be from 1 to 12, or 0 to specify a year without a
month and day.

day.description:
Day of a month. Must be from 1 to 31 and valid for the year and month, or 0
to specify a year by itself or a year and month where the day isn't
significant.

timestampVal.description:
Timestamp value to store the timestamp for the case of the date and
time data type.

List.description: Store list of values in a column.

DataAccessScope.description:
A DataAccessScope is a boolean expression of data access labels used
to restrict access to data for users.

name.description:
The unique full name of the data access scope.
The name should comply with https://google.aip.dev/122 standards.

allowedDataAccessLabels.description:
Optional. The allowed labels for the scope.
Either allow_all or allowed_data_access_labels needs to be provided.
When provided, there has to be at least one label allowed for the scope to
be valid.
The logical operator for evaluation of the allowed labels is OR.
E.g.: A customer with scope with allowed labels A and B will be able
to see data with labeled with A or B or (A and B).

deniedDataAccessLabels.description:
Optional. The denied labels for the scope.
The logical operator for evaluation of the denied labels is AND.
E.g.: A customer with scope with denied labels A and B won't be able
to see data labeled with A and data labeled with B
and data with labels A and B.

displayName.description: Output only. The name to be used for display to customers of the data access scope.

createTime.description: Output only. The time at which the data access scope was created.

updateTime.description: Output only. The time at which the data access scope was last updated.

author.description: Output only. The user who created the data access scope.

lastEditor.description: Output only. The user who last updated the data access scope.

description.description: Optional. A description of the data access scope for a human reader.

allowAll.description:
Optional. Whether or not the scope allows all labels, allow_all and
allowed_data_access_labels are mutually exclusive and one of them must be
present. denied_data_access_labels can still be used along with allow_all.
When combined with denied_data_access_labels, access will be granted to all
data that doesn't have labels mentioned in denied_data_access_labels. E.g.:
A customer with scope with denied labels A and B and allow_all will be able
to see all data except data labeled with A and data labeled with B and data
with labels A and B.

MessageSet.description:
This is proto2's version of MessageSet.

DEPRECATED: DO NOT USE FOR NEW FIELDS.

If you are using editions or proto2, please make your own extendable messages
for your use case.
If you are using proto3, please use `Any` instead.

MessageSet was the implementation of extensions for proto1.
When proto2 was introduced, extensions were implemented as a first-class
feature.
This schema for MessageSet was meant to be a "bridge" solution to migrate
MessageSet-bearing messages from proto1 to proto2.

This schema has been open-sourced only to facilitate the migration of
Google products with MessageSet-bearing messages to open-source environments.

DynamicParameter.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
DynamicParameter - Chronicle SOAR Dynamic Parameter.
Dynamic Parameters allows you to create "groups" or "categories" of
environments to further help categorize and define environments. This can
come in particularly useful when running Playbooks as you can use Custom
Trigger/Actions or Conditions based on one of these new environment
parameters. This provides greater customization for Playbooks and is useful
for Enterprises or MSSPs with lots of tenants.

x-google-enum-descriptions: The parameter is a list of strings.

EnrichmentCombination.description:
EnrichmentCombination resource represents the combination of enrichment. It
is a singleton resource that contains all the enrichment combination records.

Format:
projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/enrichmentCombination

enrichmentCombinationRecords.description: Output only. All the enrichment combination records.

EnrichmentCombinationRecord.description: EnrichmentCombinationRecord represents a record of enrichment combination.

EnrichmentSource.description:
The enrichment source that an enrichment is from. For source that comes from
external and does not have a log type, for example, `GEO_IP_SERVICE`, the
`ExternalEnrichmentSource` field should be set.

externalEnrichmentSource.description:
Required. Represents an enrichment source from external that does not have a log
type. For example, GEO_IP_SERVICE for enrichment from the GeoIP service.

RiskConfig.description:
Configurations used for the computation of Entity Risk Scores in given
instance.

name.description:
Since this is a singleton resource, name includes the name of their parent
followed by one static-segment e.g.  '.../instances/12345/riskConfig'

defaultWeightingFactor.description: Default Weighting Factor for Risk Scoring algortihms.

Environment.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
An environment is simply another word for your different networks or
customers that are managed by the SOC or MSSP. This is useful for SOCs who
provide services to several different networks, customers or business units
within the organization. The Platform comes with a predefined environment
named Default Environment.

displayName.description:
Required. Immutable. Replaces the "Name" prop with "ResourceName".
MAX_NAME_LENGTH = 256

description.description:
Required. MAX_NAME_LENGTH = 256
Description of the environment.

contact.description:
Required. MAX_NAME_LENGTH = 256
Name of the contact for the environment.

contactEmails.description:
Required. MAX_NAME_LENGTH = 256
Email of the contact for the environment.

contactPhone.description:
Required. MAX_NAME_LENGTH = 256
Phone number of the contact for the environment.

retentionDuration.description: Required. Environment data retention in months.

dynamicParameters.description:
Required. Additional custom properties for enriching the environment.
For example:
 {
  "value": "value1",
  "dynamic_parameter_id": 123,
  "environment_id": 456
 }

system.description: Required. Output only. Whether the environment is a system environment.

weight.description:
Optional. The weight of the environment, enabling customers to control distribution
of resources between the separate environments in a single instance of
Chronicle SOAR.
Environments with higher weights get prioritized for processing in
Chronicle SOAR via a lottery algorithm. Defaults to 1.

value.description: Required. The value of the dynamic parameter.

dynamicParameterId.description: Required. The ID of the dynamic parameter.

environmentId.description: Required. The ID of the environment.

If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

Total number of Environments includes filter without
pagination.

IntegrationJobInstance.description:
Allows saving & restoring different instances of job definitions.
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
Job instance resource describes the job's instances.
Job instance is a configured instance of a job definition.
The job instance is the actual timed script that runs every X seconds.

name.description:
Identifier. The unique name(ID) of the job instance.
Format:
projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/integrations/{integration}/jobs/{job}/jobInstances/{jobInstance}"

createTime.description: Output only. Job instance creation time.

updateTime.description: Output only. Job instance update time.

custom.description: Output only. Determines whether the job is custom or commercial.

enabled.description: Required. Determines whether the job instance is enabled.

displayName.description: Required. Job instance display name.

description.description: Optional. Job instance description.

intervalSeconds.description:
Required. Job instance interval in seconds.
Minimum of 60 seconds.

author.description: Output only. Job instance author.

lastRunStatus.description: Output only. Job instance last run status.

lastRunTime.description:
Output only. Job instance last execution time.
In unix time.

agent.description:
Optional. Agent identifier in case the job runs remotely, otherwise will be null.
This cannot be patched and must be defined when creating the instance.

parameters.description: Optional. Job instance parameters.

job.description: Output only. The job definition this instance belongs to.

integration.description: Output only. The integration this job instance is associated with.

advanced.description: Required. Indicates if the job instance uses an advanced scheduling configuration.

advancedConfig.description: Optional. Advanced configuration for the job instance.

nextScheduledRunTime.description:
Output only. Job instance next scheduled run time.
In unix time.

uniqueIdentifier.description: Output only. Unique identifier of the job instance.

documentationLink.description:
Output only. A URL linking to the official Google Cloud documentation for the specific
job instance.

id.description: Output only. The parameter's id.

value.description: Optional. The value of the parameter.

displayName.description: Output only. The parameter display name.

type.description: Output only. The parameter's type.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Values as semi-colon separated string parameter type.

AdvancedConfig.description:
AdvancedConfig defines detailed scheduling options for an integration job
instance. It allows specifying the time zone, and one of several schedule
types such as one-time, daily, weekly, or monthly recurrences.

oneTimeSchedule.description: Optional. The one time schedule details.

startDate.description: Required. The date to run the job.

time.description: Required. The time to run the job.

TimeOfDay.description:
Represents a time of day. The date and time zone are either not significant
or are specified elsewhere. An API may choose to allow leap seconds. Related
types are google.type.Date and `google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

hours.description:
Hours of a day in 24 hour format. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and
typically must be less than or equal to 23. An API may choose to allow the
value "24:00:00" for scenarios like business closing time.

minutes.description:
Minutes of an hour. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and less than or
equal to 59.

seconds.description:
Seconds of a minute. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and typically must
be less than or equal to 59. An API may allow the value 60 if it allows
leap-seconds.

nanos.description:
Fractions of seconds, in nanoseconds. Must be greater than or equal to 0
and less than or equal to 999,999,999.

time.description: Required. The time to run the job.

days.description: Required. The days of the week to run the job.

time.description: Required. The time to run the job.

day.description: Required. The day of the month to run the job.

time.description: Required. The time to run the job.

Integration Managers provide reusable code utilities (functions, classes,

Integration.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
Integration - Chronicle SOAR integration to a third party application.
Types of Integration:
Commercial - developed by Google Security Operations
Community - published by users
Custom - created by customer

Output only. Determines if the integration is a custom integration or commercial
integration.

certified.description:
Output only. Determines if the integration is Chronicle certified or not.
Custom integrations are not certified.
Community integrations can be certified even when not commercial.

InvolvedEntity.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
This resource represents entities that are associated with both cases and
caseAlerts, or with all caseAlerts related to a specific case. These entities
are uniquely identified by their entityIdentifier and type. Additionally, we
have another entity resource in our system that is not connected to cases and
caseAlerts - 'Unique Entity' and represents every unique entity in the
system. Unique entities is a separate collection of all distinct entities in
the system, not in the context of case/alert. Unique entities get updated (if
needed)  during ingestion when new entities arrive.

id.description: Required. Immutable. The id of the entity.

type.description: Required. Immutable. The type of the entity. Could be: HOSTNAME, USB, PROCESS, ADDRESS, …

suspicious.description: Required. Describes if the entity is suspicious or not.

internal.description:
Required. Indicates whether this entity is internal. This setting is configured in
the application's settings, and any entity type can be designated as
internal.

threatSource.description: Optional. Threat source of the entity.

operatingSystem.description: Optional. Operating system related to the entity.

networkTitle.description: Optional. Network name related to the entity.

networkPriority.description: Optional. Network priority of a related network.

attacker.description: Optional. Describes if the entity represents an attacker or not.

pivot.description:
Optional. Describes if the entity is a pivot or not. A pivot entity is an entity
which is common to two or more cases. In the case graph, it is connected to
other entities by a dotted line.

environment.description: Output only. Immutable. The environment the entity belongs to. Derived from the case and alert.

manuallyCreated.description:
Output only. Immutable. Describes if the entity was created by the system (as part of the ingestion
flow), or manually by the user.

additionalProperties.description: Optional. Stores additional properties, as a JSON format.

sourceSystemUri.description: Optional. Output only. Immutable. Link to the source system.

enriched.description:
Output only. Indicates whether the entity has been enriched. An entity is enriched when
an action adds information from an external system.

artifact.description: Output only. Describes if the entity is an artifact or not.

vulnerable.description: Output only. Describes if the entity is vulnerable or not.

entityUri.description:
Optional. Output only. The full url of the entity, calculated using the source_system_uri and the
entity data. Optional if the source_system_uri is not defined.

fields.description:
Output only. A list of context group items, which are calculated based on the entity's
properties.

alertIdentifier.description:
Output only. The alert identifier of the alert that the involved entity is associated
with.

caseId.description: Output only. The id of the case that the involved entity is associated with.

identifier.description: Output only. The identifier name of the involved entity.

displayName.description: Output only. The name of the context group. Default context group is called "Default".

highlighted.description: Output only. Is the context group highlighted.

hidden.description: Output only. Is the context group hidden.

items.description: Output only. A list of items in the group.

ContextGroupItem.description: A single item in the context group.

name.description: Output only. The name of the property

originalName.description: Output only. The original name of the property.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description: The total number of InvolvedEntities.

key.description: Required. The existing property key, whose value should be updated.

identifier.description: Required. The identifier of the entity.

type.description: Required. The type of the entity.

x-google-enum-descriptions: Both inbound and outbound security entity direction.

JobInstanceLog.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
Describes a integration job's instance log.

startTime.description:
Output only. Job execution start time.
Start time is UTC time, which is a time reflected as seconds since the
Unix epoch.

endTime.description:
Output only. Job execution end time.
Start time is UTC time, which is a time reflected as seconds since the
Unix epoch.

jobIdentifier.description: Output only. The identifier of the job.

jobInstanceId.description: Output only. The job instance id.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description: Output only. Total number of JobInstanceLogs.

LabsExperiment.description: The LabsExperiment resource, contains the metadata of an experiment.

name.description: Identifier. The unique name of the Experiment.

description.description: Output only. The description of the Experiment.

category.description: Output only. The category of the experiment. (e.g. Detections, Search)

createTime.description: Output only. The time the experiment was created, also used as a Release Date.

updateTime.description: Output only. The time the experiment was last updated.

expiryTime.description: Output only. The time the experiment expires, UI representation only.

metadata.description: Output only. Metadata for the experiment, such as image.

timeout.description: Output only. Timeout for the entire experiment execution

uiSchema.description:
Output only. The UI schema of the experiment defining the experiment page.
This is used when type is STANDARD.

enabled.description: Optional. Whether the experiment is enabled.

type.description:
Output only. The type of the experiment. This determines which configuration
(e.g., ui_schema or agentic_config) is relevant.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The traditional multi-stage flow (Form/Chat stages) driven by ui_schema.
The ADK-based, single-session flow driven by agentic_config.

agenticConfig.description:
Output only. Configuration for Agentic experiments.
This is used when type is `AGENTIC`.

agentId.description: Output only. The ID of the Agent in the Agent Hub (for routing)

welcomeMessage.description:
Output only. A simple text message shown immediately to the user upon opening the
experiment.

initialPrompt.description: Output only. A prompt sent to the agent automatically when the session starts.

nextPageToken.description:
Optional. A token to retrieve the next page of results. AIP-132
If this field is empty, there are no subsequent pages.

LabsExperimentExecution.description:
The experiment execution resource, contains execution context and results
of an experiment.

name.description:
Identifier. The unique identifier of the ExperimentExecution.
As this is a child of an Experiment, the name of the Experiment is
included in the name.
Format:
projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/labsExperiments/{labs_experiment}/executions/{labs_experiment_execution}

createTime.description: Output only. When the execution request was initially received by the Labs Service.

endTime.description:
Output only. When the execution reached a terminal state (SUCCESS, FAILURE, TIMEOUT,
etc.). nil if the execution is still in progress. Output only.

lastUpdateTime.description:
Output only. When the execution state (status, message) was last modified.
Useful for tracking progress and concurrency control.

state.description: Output only. The current lifecycle state of the execution.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Execution is queued and waiting to be processed.
Execution is actively being processed by a worker.
Execution completed successfully and produced a result.

Execution failed due to an error within the experiment logic or
infrastructure issue.
Executin failed because it exceeded the configured timeout limit.
Execution was cancelled (e.g., by user request).

stateMessage.description:
Output only. An optional, user-friendly message describing the current state or
progress. Example: "Processing step 2/3: Calling external API..."

context.description:
Output only. The final result payload if the execution completed successfully (state is
SUCCESS). Stored as a generic Struct to accommodate varied structures from
different experiments.

LabsExperimentExecutionContext.description:
The execution context for an Experiment.
Defines inputs, outputs, and other metadata for an Experiment execution.

stages.description:
Output only. The stages of the execution.
This is used when type is `STANDARD`.

agenticContext.description:
Output only. Context for AGENTIC experiments.
This is used when type is`AGENTIC`.

ExperimentExecutionStage.description: A definition of a stage in the execution.

name.description: Required. The unique identifier of the stage.

current.description: Optional. Is the stage the current stage of the execution.

inputs.description: Required. Thze input payload of the stage.

outputs.description: Output only. The output payload of the stage.

ExperimentExecutionStageOutput.description: Defines the outputs of a stage.

text.description:
Output only. The text payload of the stage output.
Matches a markdown, Json, and code.

generativeUiSchema.description:
Output only. A UI schema for a generative UI output.
This is used to render the message in the UI.

renderType.description: Output only. The type of the stage output.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The stage output is a chat/conversation output.
The stage output is a code output.
The stage output is a JSON output.
The stage output is a generative UI output.

name.description: Output only. The unique name/identifier for the output of the stage output.

sessionId.description: Output only. Metadata related to the Agent Hub.

messages.description: Output only. The history of messages in the agentic session.

LabsChatMessage.description: Represents a single message in a chat conversation.

role.description: Output only. The role of the message sender. Allowed values are "user", and "agent".

text.description: Required. The text content of the message.

visibility.description: Output only. The visibility state of the message.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Message is hidden from the UI.

generativeUiSchema.description:
Output only. Generative UI representation of the message.
This is used to render the message in the UI.

labsExperimentExecutions.description: The list of LabsExperimentExecution resources found.

nextPageToken.description: A token to retrieve the next page of results.

context.description:
Required. The input payload provided by the user when initiating the execution.
Stored as a generic Struct for flexibility.
The specific structure exists in the existing service implementation.

executionId.description:
Optional. The unique identifier of the execution.
If not provided, a random UUID will be generated.

options.description: Optional. Options for executing a Labs experiment.

uiGenerationEnabled.description: Optional. If true, generative UI schema will be generated and returned in response.

ExecuteLabsExperimentMetadata.description: Metadata for the ExecuteLabsExperiment long-running operation.

state.description: Output only. The current lifecycle state of the execution.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Execution is queued and waiting to be processed.
Execution is actively being processed by a worker.
Execution completed successfully and produced a result.

Execution failed due to an error within the experiment logic or
infrastructure issue.
Executin failed because it exceeded the configured timeout limit.
Execution was cancelled (e.g., by user request).

createTime.description: Output only. When the execution request was initially received by the Labs Service.

Rule.description: The Rule resource represents a user-created rule.

name.description:
Identifier. Full resource name for the rule.
Format:
`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/rules/{rule}`

revisionId.description:
Output only. The revision ID of the rule.
A new revision is created whenever the rule text is changed in any way.
Format: `v_{10 digits}_{9 digits}`
Populated in REVISION_METADATA_ONLY view and FULL view.

displayName.description:
Output only. Display name of the rule.
Populated in BASIC view and FULL view.

text.description:
The YARA-L content of the rule.
Populated in FULL view.

author.description:
Output only. The author of the rule. Extracted from the meta section of text.
Populated in BASIC view and FULL view.

severity.description:
Output only. The severity of the rule as specified in the meta section of text.
Populated in BASIC view and FULL view.

metadata.description:
Output only. Additional metadata specified in the meta section of text.
Populated in FULL view.

createTime.description:
Output only. The timestamp of when the rule was created.
Populated in FULL view.

revisionCreateTime.description:
Output only. The timestamp of when the rule revision was created.
Populated in FULL, REVISION_METADATA_ONLY views.

compilationState.description:
Output only. The current compilation state of the rule.
Populated in FULL view.
x-google-enum-descriptions:
The Rule cannot successfully compile.
This is possible if a backwards-incompatible change was made to the
compiler.

type.description:
Output only. User-facing type of the rule. Extracted from the events section of rule
text. Populated in BASIC view and FULL view.

referenceLists.description:
Output only. Resource names of the reference lists used in this rule.
Populated in FULL view.

allowedRunFrequencies.description:
Output only. The run frequencies that are allowed for the rule.
Populated in BASIC view and FULL view.

etag.description:
The etag for this rule.
If this is provided on update, the request will succeed if and only if it
matches the server-computed value, and will fail with an ABORTED error
otherwise.
Populated in BASIC view and FULL view.

scope.description:
Resource name of the DataAccessScope bound to this rule.
Populated in BASIC view and FULL view.
If reference lists are used in the rule, validations will be performed
against this scope to ensure that the reference lists are compatible with
both the user's and the rule's scopes.
The scope should be in the format:
`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/dataAccessScopes/{scope}`.

compilationDiagnostics.description:
Output only. A list of a rule's corresponding compilation diagnostic messages
such as compilation errors and compilation warnings.
Populated in FULL view.

nearRealTimeLiveRuleEligible.description:
Output only. Indicate the rule can run in near real time live rule.
If this is true, the rule uses the near real time live rule when the run
frequency is set to LIVE.

inputsUsed.description:
Output only. The set of inputs used in the rule. For example, if the rule uses
$e.principal.hostname, then the uses_udm field will be true.

Severity.description: Severity represents the severity level of the rule.

displayName.description:
The display name of the severity level. Extracted from the meta section of
the rule text.

CompilationDiagnostic.description:
CompilationDiagnostic represents a compilation diagnostic generated
during a rule's compilation, such as a compilation error or a compilation
warning.

position.description:
Output only. The approximate position in the rule text associated with the
compilation diagnostic.
Compilation Position may be empty.

severity.description: Output only. The severity of a rule's compilation diagnostic.

uri.description: Output only. Link to documentation that describes a diagnostic in more detail.

CompilationPosition.description:
CompilationPosition represents the location of a compilation diagnostic in
rule text.

startLine.description: Output only. Start line number, beginning at 1.

startColumn.description: Output only. Start column number, beginning at 1.

endLine.description: Output only. End line number, beginning at 1.

endColumn.description: Output only. End column number, beginning at 1.

InputsUsed.description:
InputsUsed is a convenience field that tells us which sources
of events (if any) were used in the rule.

usesUdm.description: Optional. Whether the rule queries UDM events.

usesEntity.description: Optional. Whether the rule queries entity events.

usesDetection.description: Optional. Whether the rule queries detections.

rules.description: The rules from the specified instance.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description:
An estimate of the total number of rules that match the query.
This value is only populated when the `view` is `TRENDS`.

ruleText.description: Required. The rule text to verify as a UTF-8 string.

success.description: Whether or not the rule text was successfully verified.

compilationDiagnostics.description:
A list of a rule's corresponding compilation diagnostic messages
such as compilation errors and compilation warnings.

nextPageToken.description:
A token that can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

Retrohunt.description: Retrohunt is an execution of a Rule over a time range in the past.

name.description:
The resource name of the retrohunt.
Retrohunt is the child of a rule revision. {rule} in the format below is
structured as {rule_id@revision_id}.
Format:
`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/rules/{rule}/retrohunts/{retrohunt}`

processInterval.description: Required. The start and end time of the event time range this retrohunt processes.

executionInterval.description:
Output only. The start and end time of the retrohunt execution. If the retrohunt is not
yet finished, the end time of the interval will not be populated.

state.description: Output only. The state of the retrohunt.

progressPercentage.description: Output only. Percent progress of the retrohunt towards completion, from 0.00 to 100.00.

retrohunt.description:
The name of the retrohunt.
Format:
`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/rules/{rule}/retrohunts/{retrohunt}`

executionInterval.description:
The start and end time of the retrohunt execution. If the retrohunt is not
yet finished, the end time of the interval will not be filled.

progressPercentage.description: Percent progress of the retrohunt towards completion, from 0.00 to 100.00.

retrohunts.description: The retrohunts from the specified rule.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

RuleDeployment.description: The RuleDeployment resource represents the deployment state of a Rule.

name.description:
Identifier. The resource name of the rule deployment.
Note that RuleDeployment is a child of the overall Rule, not any individual
revision, so the resource ID segment for the Rule resource must not
reference a specific revision.
Format:
`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/rules/{rule}/deployment`

enabled.description: Whether the rule is currently deployed continuously against incoming data.

alerting.description:
Whether detections resulting from this deployment should be considered
alerts.

archived.description:
The archive state of the rule deployment.
Cannot be set to true unless enabled is set to false.
If set to true, alerting will automatically be set to false.
If currently set to true, enabled, alerting, and run_frequency cannot be
updated.

archiveTime.description:
Output only. The timestamp when the rule deployment archive state was last set to true.
If the rule deployment's current archive state is not set to true,
the field will be empty.

runFrequency.description: The run frequency of the rule deployment.

executionState.description: Output only. The execution state of the rule deployment.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Rules in limited state may not have their executions guaranteed.
Paused rules are not executed at all.

producerRules.description:
Output only. The names of the associated/chained producer rules. Rules are considered
producers for this rule if this rule explicitly filters on their ruleid.
Format:
`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/rules/{rule}`

consumerRules.description:
Output only. The names of the associated/chained consumer rules. Rules are considered
consumers of this rule if their rule text explicitly filters on this rule's
ruleid.
Format:
`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/rules/{rule}`

lastAlertStatusChangeTime.description:
Output only. The timestamp when the rule deployment alert state was lastly changed.
This is filled regardless of the current alert state.
E.g. if the current alert status is false, this timestamp will be the
timestamp when the alert status was changed to false.

ruleDeployments.description: The rule deployments from all rules.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

Parser.description: Parser converts a raw log to UDM Events.

cbn.description:
if the parser is built using config
documentation:
https://cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/preview/parser-extensions/parsing-overview

lowCode.description: if the parser is built using the low code feature.

releaseStage.description:
Output only. The release stage of the parser
After internal validations the prebuilt parser will directly start as
Release Candidate. The release_stage of prebuilt parsers are changed
after every release cycle:
The prebuilt Release Candidate parser is promoted as Release parser.
The existing prebuilt Release parser is moved to Rollback state.
and existing prebuilt rollback parser is moved to Archived.
In case of custom parser:
When the customer submits a validation passed custom parser it starts as
Release state.
And existing one is moved to Rollback stage.
And the existing rollback is moved to Archived.
In case a release or release candidate parser is found faulty,
the parser is marked FAULTY,
if it is release parser then rollback candidate is moved to release.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The release stage is not specified.
The parser is RELEASED and can be used in production.
The prebuilt parser is a release candidate.
The prebuilt parser is a rollback candidate.
The parser has been marked faulty, and is available for auto deletion.

Archived parser which is currently in use.
This is possible if customer has pinned parser which was later archived.

validationStage.description:
Output only. The validation stage of the parser
When a customer submits a new parser for validation, it starts with a
new stage.
When parser is picked for validation, it changes to Validation state.
If validation failed it is marked as failed, and
existing failed is moved to delete_candidate stage.
If passes it is moved to passed stage.
If customer opts to submit it, the parser is moved to Release State.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The validation stage is not specified.
The custom parser is submitted for validation.
The custom parser is currently going through the validation pipeline
The custom parser has successfully passed the validation.
The custom parser has failed validation.
The parser is no good, It is available for auto deletion.

name.description: Output only. name of the parser resource.

creator.description: Output only. Creator of the parser.

createTime.description: Output only. Time at which parser was created.

changelogs.description: Output only. Represents all the changes made into this parser.

parserExtension.description: Output only. Extension applied over the parser, if any.

dynamicParsingConfig.description: Output only. Dynamic parsing config applied over the parser, if any.

type.description: Output only. The type of the parser
x-google-enum-descriptions: The parser is specific to a customer or partner.
The parser is created and managed by Google Cloud in Chronicle.

state.description: Output only. The state of the parser

validationReport.description: Output only. The Validation report generated during parser validation.

validatedOnEmptyLogs.description:
Optional. Flag to bypass parser validation when no logs are found.
If enabled, the parser won't be be rejected during the validation
phase when no logs are found.

versionInfo.description: Optional. The version information of the parser.

validationSkipped.description:
Optional. If true, bypasses parser validation.
If enabled, the parser won't be rejected during the validation
phase and validation will be skipped.

fieldExtractors.description: Represents the mapping logic of input to output.

log.description: The log used to create this low code parser in the UI.

FieldExtractors.description: A representation of a parser extension as a set of field extractors.

transformedCbnSnippet.description: Output only. CBN snippet generated from field extractors.

logFormat.description: Format of the log. Ex. CSV,JSON,XML.

appendRepeatedFields.description:
Whether to append repeated fields or not.
When false, repeated fields will be replaced.

FieldExtractor.description: An extractor for a single log field.

preconditionPath.description:
Precondition path could be a json path, xml path or csv column name
depending on log format. It refers to a section or substring in raw log.

fieldPath.description:
Field path could be a json path, xml path or csv column name
depending on log format. It refers to a section or substring in raw log.
This is required if the FieldExtractor is used to specify the parser
extension.

destinationPath.description:
Path in generated event which is to be populated. This is required if the
FieldExtractor is used to specify the parser extension.

value.description: Value to be mapped to the destination path directly.

PreProcessConfig.description: PreProcessConfig holds the GROK expression to extract the syslog header.

grokRegex.description:
GROK Regex to extract the structured part of the log.
syntax documentation:
www.elastic.co/guide/en/logstash/current/plugins-filters-grok.html

target.description:
Target field name for the structured part of the log.
This should match a SEMANTIC identifier from the grok expression.

Creator.description: Information about the creator of the parser.

customer.description:
Output only. The customer who created it, This can represent the partner as well.
In case of prebuilt parser this will be empty.

author.description: Output only. The name of the author, who created this parser.

source.description: Output only. The source of the parser.
x-google-enum-descriptions: A prebuilt parser, present in Google's system.

The custom parser copied from a prebuilt parser and present in Google's
system.
The custom parser, made by a partner.
The customer parser, made by the customer.

entries.description: all the changelog of a parser.

createTime.description: Time at which changelog was created.

deleted.description:
Optional. Flag whether the entry is added or deleted.
This will be true in case of rollback and false in case of upgrade.

parserVersion.description: Optional. The parser version for which the changelog is created.

ParserVersionInfo.description:
ParserVersionInfo gives the version information of the parser and related
properties like pinned etc.

version.description: Output only. The version of the parser.

autoUpgradeDisabled.description:
Required. Signifies if the parser is disabled for auto upgrade. If true, the parser
will not be upgraded by the auto upgrade process.

rollbackAvailable.description: Output only. Signifies if rollback is available for this parser version.

latestParserVersion.description: Output only. The version for the latest available stable version of the parser.

parsers.description:
The parsers from the specified chronicle instance.
When the view is set to `BASIC_VIEW` in the request, the `config`,
`changelogs`, `parser_extension`, and `dynamic_parsing_config` fields will
not be populated for each parser. See `ParserView` for more details on the
different views.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

log.description: sample logs used for while running the parser

statedumpAllowed.description:
Optional. Flag whether the Statedump filter is enabled or not for a config.
If enabled, the intermediate state of the parsing will be returned as part
of the output. Useful for debugging purposes.

ParserExtension.description: ParserExtension provides extensibility semantics for a log parser.

cbnSnippet.description: Parser config could be a cbn snippet.

fieldExtractors.description: Parser config could be a field extractor.

dynamicParsing.description: Parser config could be a dynamic parsing config.

name.description:
Output only. Format:
projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/logTypes/{logtype}/parserExtensions/{parserExtension}

state.description: Output only. The state of the parser extension
x-google-enum-descriptions: The state for this parser extension was not specified.

The parser extension has been newly submitted and is waiting to be
validated.

The parser extension is currently going through the validation
pipeline.

The parser extension is live in production. Only configs that have
