Validation completed, but the parser extension was rejected with
errors.
An error occurred when processing this parser extension.
Extension is archived and is no more being used.

createTime.description: Output only. The time the parser extension was created.

log.description: Raw log used to assist the user in creation of augmentation.

extensionValidationReport.description:
The latest extension
validation report for this extension.

validationReport.description: Output only. The validation report generated during extension validation.

stateLastChangedTime.description: The time the config state was last changed.

lastLiveTime.description: The time the config was last serving live traffic.

validationSkipped.description:
Optional. Flag to bypass parser extension validation.
If enabled, the parser extension won't be rejected during the validation
phase and validation will be skipped.

DynamicParsingConfig.description: A representation of a parser extension as dynamic parsing config.

optedFields.description: Optional. List of fields to be parsed.

LogField.description: A representation of a log field.

path.description: Optional. Path of the log field.

sampleValue.description: Optional. Sample value of the log field.

runParserResults.description: Results produced after running a parser over log.

ParserLogResult.description: Result generated when normalizing a log.

parsedEvents.description: Parsed events when normalizing a log.

error.description: Error message when normalizing the log.

ParsedEvents.description: Normalized events generated from a log.

events.description: Events generated when normalizing the log.

ParsedEvent.description: EventInfo is a wrapper containing a UDM or Entity.

metadata.description: Event metadata such as timestamp, source product, etc.

additional.description:
Any important vendor-specific event data that cannot be adequately
represented within the formal sections of the UDM model.

principal.description:
Represents the acting entity that originates the activity
described in the event. The principal must include at least one machine
detail (hostname, MACs, IPs, port, product-specific identifiers like an
EDR asset ID) or user detail (for example, username), and optionally
include process details. It must NOT include any of the following fields:
email, files, registry keys or values.

src.description:
Represents a source entity being acted upon by the participant along with
the device or process context for the source object (the machine where the
source object resides). For example, if user U copies file A on machine X
to file B on machine Y, both file A and machine X would be specified in the
src portion of the UDM event.

target.description:
Represents a target entity being referenced by the event or an object on
the target entity. For example, in a firewall connection from device A to
device B, A is described as the principal and B is described as the target.
For a process injection by process C into target process D, process C is
described as the principal and process D is described as the target.

intermediary.description:
Represents details on one or more intermediate entities processing activity
described in the event. This includes device details about a proxy server
or SMTP relay server. If an active event (that has a principal and
possibly target) passes through any intermediaries, they're added here.
Intermediaries can impact the overall action, for example blocking or
modifying an ongoing request.  A rule of thumb here is that 'principal',
'target', and description of the initial action should be the same
regardless of the intermediary or its action.  A successful network
connection from A->B should look the same in principal/target/intermediary
as one blocked by firewall C: principal: A, target: B (intermediary: C).

observer.description:
Represents an observer entity (for example, a packet sniffer or
network-based vulnerability scanner), which is not a direct intermediary,
but which observes and reports on the event in question.

about.description:
Represents entities referenced by the event that are not otherwise
described in principal, src, target, intermediary or observer. For example,
it could be used to track email file attachments, domains/URLs/IPs embedded
within an email body, and DLLs that are loaded during a PROCESS_LAUNCH
event.

network.description:
All network details go here, including sub-messages with details on each
protocol (for example, DHCP, DNS, or HTTP).

extensions.description:
All other first-class, event-specific metadata goes in this message.
Do not place protocol metadata in Extensions; put it in Network.

extracted.description: Flattened fields extracted from the log.

grouped.description: Related UDM fields that are grouped together.

linuxUtmp.description:
A Linux Utmp extension. This captures details specific to Linux Utmp
events, which record login and logout sessions on a Linux system.

windowsEventLog.description:
A Windows Event Log extension. This captures details specific to Windows
Event Log events, providing structured information from various Windows
logs.

resourceUsage.description:
A resource usage extension. This captures details about what entity
(e.g., process, user) is using a specific resource.

systemEventDetails.description:
A system event details extension. This captures additional details for
system-level events, such as message type, sender image ID, and subsystem.

outlookMetadata.description:
A Microsoft Outlook specific metadata extension. This includes metadata
related to Outlook items, such as comments, templates, and security flags.

srum.description:
A SRUM extension. This captures details specific to Windows System Resource
Usage Monitor (SRUM) events, providing insights into application resource
consumption.

userAssist.description:
A UserAssist extension. This captures details specific to Windows User
Assist events, which track application usage and execution.

Authentication.description:
The Authentication extension captures details specific to authentication
events.
General guidelines for authentication events:
* Details about the source of the authentication event (for example, client
  IP or hostname), should be captured in principal. The principal may be
  empty if we have no details about the source of the login.
* Details about the target of the authentication event (for example, details
  about the machine that is being logged into or logged out of) should be
  captured in target.
* Some authentication events may involve a third-party. For example, a user
  logs into
  a cloud service (for example, Chronicle) via their company's SSO (the
  event is logged by their SSO solution). In this case, the principal
  captures information about the user's device, the target captures details
  about the cloud service they logged into, and the intermediary captures
  details about the SSO solution.

type.description: The type of authentication.
x-google-enum-descriptions:
A TACACS family protocol for networked systems authentication
(e.g. TACACS, TACACS+).
Some other mechanism that is not defined here.
Cached Remote Interactive authentication using cached credentials.
Cached Remote Interactive authentication using cached credentials.
Biometric device such as a fingerprint reader.
Wearable such as an Apple Watch.

authDetails.description: The vendor defined details of the authentication.

outcome.description: The outcome of the authentication event.

Vulnerabilities.description:
The Vulnerabilities extension captures details on observed/detected
vulnerabilities.

LinuxUtmp.description: The LinuxUtmp extension captures details specific to Linux Utmp events.

x-google-enum-descriptions: New time after system clock change.
Old time before system clock change.

WindowsEventLog.description:
The WindowsEventLog extension captures details specific to Windows Event Log
events.

eventId.description: A unique identifier for a specific type of event.

activityId.description:
A GUID (Globally Unique Identifier) used to link a sequence of related
events together.

ResourceUsage.description: The ResourceUsage extension captures details about what is using a resource.

usedEntity.description: The name of the entity (e.g., process, user) that is using the resource.

usedEntityId.description: A numerical identifier for the entity using the resource (e.g., PID, UID).

SystemEventDetails.description: Captures additional details for system-level events.

messageType.description: The specific type or category of the message.

senderImageId.description: An identifier for the image associated with the sender of the message.

subsystem.description: The subsystem or component that generated the event.

comment.description: A user-defined comment or note associated with the Outlook item.

template.description: The name of the template file used to create the Outlook item.

title.description: The title of the Outlook item.

securityFlagsCount.description:
Count of Security-related flags for the message, such as encryption or
signing status.

Srum.description:
The Srum extension captures details specific to Windows System Resource
Usage Monitor (SRUM) events.

id.description:
A unique identifier for the SRUM record or the application/user being
monitored.

backgroundBytesRead.description:
The number of bytes read by the application while running in the
background.

backgroundBytesWritten.description:
The number of bytes written by the application while running in the
background.

backgroundContextSwitches.description:
The number of context switches performed by the application's threads
while in the background.

backgroundCycleCount.description:
The amount of CPU cycle time consumed by the application in the
background, measured in clock cycles.

backgroundFlushesCount.description:
The number of flush operations performed by the application in the
background.

backgroundReadOperations.description:
The number of read operations performed by the application in the
background.

backgroundWriteOperations.description:
The number of write operations performed by the application in the
background.

interfaceLuid.description:
The Locally Unique Identifier (LUID) for the network interface used for
data transfer.

UserAssist.description:
The UserAssist extension captures details specific to Windows User Assist
events.

applicationFocusCount.description:
The number of times the application associated with the entry gained
focus.

applicationFocusDuration.description: The total duration the application associated with the entry was in focus.

executionsCount.description:
The number of times the application associated with the entry has been
executed.

entryIndex.description: The index or identifier of the user assist entry, unique per user.

GroupedFields.description:
Grouped fields are aliases for groups of related UDM fields. All
fields grouped together are of type string.

BackstoryEntity.description:
An Entity provides additional context about an item in a UDM event. For
example, a PROCESS_LAUNCH event describes that user 'abc@example.corp'
launched process 'shady.exe'.
The event does not include information that user 'abc@example.com' is a
recently terminated employee who administers a server storing finance data.
Information stored in one or more Entities can add this additional context.

metadata.description: Entity metadata such as timestamp, product, etc.

entity.description: Noun in the UDM event that this entity represents.

relations.description:
One or more relationships between the entity (a) and other entities,
including the relationship type and related entity.

additional.description:
Important entity data that cannot be adequately represented within
the formal sections of the Entity.

riskScore.description: Stores information related to the entity's risk score.

metric.description:
Stores statistical metrics about the entity. Used if metadata.entity_type
is METRIC.

StatedumpResult.description: StatedumpResult represents statedump filter output generated from a log.

label.description: Label represents a tag for the statedump filter.

statedumpResult.description: StatedumpResult represents output for the corresponding label.

ValidationReport describes the validation status of a parser/extension when
it was created.

Stats.description:
Stats represents the statistics generated by running the parser/extension
over a set of sample logs.

successfullyNormalizedLogCount.description: Count of log entries which successfully created valid events.

failedLogCount.description: Count of log entries which failed to create a valid event.

invalidLogCount.description:
Count of log entries which were skipped in parsing because they were
invalid.

onErrorCount.description:
Count of times "on_error" was encountered while parsing.
An `on_error` field is used in CBN config to catch an error and handle.
`on_error_count` counts the number of times errors were handled while
parsing the logs.

genericEventCount.description: Count of events created of type GENERIC_EVENT.

eventCategoryCounts.description:
Stats to account for events created.
It stores invalid event reasons and the count corresponding to it.

dropTagCounts.description:
Stats to account for number of logs dropped.
When we drop a log line from parsing, we specify a drop tag.
We tag it to identify the reason of drop.
`drop_tag_counts` stores the reason for drop and the count corresponding
to it.

maxParseDuration.description: Max time taken to parse a log entry.

avgParseDuration.description: Average parse time taken to parse a log entry.

normalizationPercentage.description:
Percentage of log lines that successfully generated at least 1 valid
event.

useArrayBracketNotation.description:
Optional. Flag to format arrays as bracket notation.
When true, arrays will be serialized with brackets surrounding the index
When false, there will be no change in the serialization of arrays
and the default dot (.) notation will be used.

compressArrayFields.description:
Optional. Flag to compress array fields.
When true, array fields will be truncated to include only the first element
and elipssis (...) will be used to indicate if it has more elements. Also
there will be no index supplied for the array elements.
When false, array fields will be serialized with all elements.

PropertySchemaDefinition.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
The properties metadata enable customers to rewrite how event fields will be
presented and under what category they appear such as case overview – event
fields and entity screen – enrichment fields. So for example, I can create a
properties metadata in the platform so that all the events or enrichment
fields that start with the VT_ prefix will be grouped under the VirusTotal
category.

rawFieldName.description:
Required. Title of the raw field - the system name.
Limited to 100 characters.

displayName.description: Required. How to display the raw_field_title on the case overview screen.

prefix.description:
Optional. Used for grouping multiple fields together.
Limited to 256 characters.

trimPrefix.description:
Optional. Whether to display the prefix as part of the field name.
Example – "VTdepartment" will be presented as "department" in case you
defined "VT" prefix and trimmed it. Default value: false

system.description:
Output only. Indicates if the field is in the system category or not.
Also inidicates if the field should be displayed in the UI screen or not.

highlighted.description:
Optional. To display the field in the Highlighted section of the cases
overview screen or not. Default value: false

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

re-enable it in the future.

loggingLevel.description: Output only. Current logging level in the remote agent.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Remote agent logging level is unspecified.
Remote agent logging level is error.
Remote agent logging level is warning.
Remote agent logging level is information.
Remote agent logging level is debug.

null, it means that it acts as a backup remote agent in case something
happens to the primary remote agent. Unique GUID identifier of the remote
agent

certificate.description: Output only. The certificate of the remote agent.

remoteAgentAvailabilityConnectorState.description:
Output only. Indicates if a remote agent is available for use
with the connector.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Remote agent connector availability state is unspecified.

Remote agent connector availability state is available for the
connector.

Remote agent connector availability state is misconfigured -
integration is not installed on the remote agent .
Remote agent connector availability state is connection error.
Integration version is not supported for this remote agent.

remoteAgentIdentifiers.description: Required. List of remote agent identifiers.

lastCommunicationTime.description: Output only. Last communication time with the remote agent in unix time.

lastActionExecutionTime.description: Output only. Last action execution time in the remote agent in unix time.

version.description: Output only. Represents the remote agent's current version.

requiredPlatformVersion.description: Output only. Required platform version to run any integration on the remote agent.

ipAddress.description: Output only. Represents the remote agent's IP address.

hostName.description: Output only. Represents the remote agent's host name.

communicationStatus.description: Output only. Indicates the remote agent's current communication status in real time.
x-google-enum-descriptions:
An agent status of 'live' means it has an active connection with the
system.

An agent status of 'error' means it does not have an active connection
with the system.

An agent status of 'disabled' means it was disabled by the user and can
be re-enabled in the future.

An agent status of 'waiting for agent' means either the system is
waiting for a connection response from the agent or the agent is
undergoing a process such as an upgrade.

highAvailabilityStatus.description:
Output only. Indicates the secondary remote agent's communication status in real time.
The secondary remote agent acts as a backup mechanism in case the primary
remote agent becomes unavailable - for example due to high load. Therefore,
it is named as HighAvailabilityStatus.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The secondary remote agent is fully operational.

There is a mismatch in versions between the remote agent and the
platform, or the status cannot be calculated.
Either the Primary or the secondary remote agent is disabled.

A secondary remote agent status of 'Pending Secondary Agent' means that
the secondary remote agent is in a pending state either because the
systme is waiting for a connection response from the remote agent or
the remote agent is undergoing a process and waiting for it to finish.
This indicates that a secondary remote agent does not exist.

usages.description:
Output only. List of all integrations, connectors and jobs installed on the remote agent
for a specific integration identifier.

deploymentType.description: Output only. Remote agent installation type
x-google-enum-descriptions: Remote agent installation type is unspecified.

This specifies that the user requests to install the new remote agent as
a docker container.

This specifies that the user requests to install the new remote agent
using the installer.

This specifies that the user requests to install the new remote agent
using a podman container.

loggingLevel.description: Output only. Logging level on the remote agent.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Remote agent logging level is unspecified.
Remote agent logging level is error.
Remote agent logging level is warning.

majorUpgrade.description:
Output only. Indicates if the remote agent's version is a major version or not. Major

deploying new docker image.

actionCodeGenerationMetadata.description: Optional. Feedback metadata specific to action code generation.

Required. The numerical rating provided by the user;
The number should be between 1 to 5.

Required. The type or category of the product being reviewed.
Maximum length: 50 characters.

Maximum length: 50 characters.
Example: "2".

productVersion.description:
Required. The version of the product being reviewed.
Maximum length: 20 characters.
Example: "1.0.0".

Required. The feedback id to be submitted.
This id is a GUID.
Example: "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000".

CaseSummaryFeedbackMetadata.description: Contains metadata specific to case summary feedback.

caseAssistantType.description: Required. Type of the assistant for the case summary.

SlaDefinition.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
A Service Level Agreement (SLA) represents a commitment by the SOC to perform
specific tasks, such as investigation or remediation of specific cases within
a specified duration of time.

slaType.description:
Required. Immutable. The SLA type.
Cannot be updated after creation.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Either all alerts or specific ones.

Case stage, defined in Case proto.
e.g. TRIAGE, INVESTIGATION. or ASSESSMENT.

Case priority, defined in Case proto. Can be one of the following
options: INFORMATIVE, LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, or CRITICAL.

Alert priority, defined in Alert proto. Can be one of the following
options: INFORMATIVE, LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, or CRITICAL.

alertType.description:
Optional. When ALERT_RULE_GENERATOR is chosen, specify which type of alert to set
SLA.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Either all alerts or specific ones.

slaTypeValue.description:
Required. Can be a specific alert-rule-generator in case
SLA_TYPE = ALERT_RULE_GENERATOR,
or CASE_STAGE = TRIAGE

slaPeriod.description: Required. Amount of time in seconds from SLA starts until SLA time expires.

slaPeriodTimeUnit.description: Required. Specify time unit for the SLA period (minutes, hours, etc.).

criticalSlaPeriod.description: Required. The time period of the SLA until it becomes critical.

criticalSlaPeriodTimeUnit.description: Required. The unit of critical sla period (minutes, hours, etc.).

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description: Total count of SlaDefinitions.

content.description: Output only. The CSV file content as bytes.
Required. The CSV file content containing SlaDefinitions to import.

overrideExistingRecords.description: Optional. Whether to override existing SlaDefinition records during import.

so that when an entity is created during the alert ingestion process, it
appears as an internal asset.

This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
Allows a customer to add, modify and delete networks in a CIDR format.
The system supports identification of soar_network subnets.

SocRole.description:
This service is available for customers who migrated SOAR to a customer
managed project and have the Chronicle API enabled.
Conventionally, different access rights are assigned to different SOC roles,
but within your organization, you can experiment with the permission levels
and environments to determine the full scope of responsibility for each user
group in the Google Security Operations platform. The Google Security
Operations platform comes with predefined SOC roles but customized roles can
be added.

displayName.description:
Required. Name of the soc-role.
MAX_NAME_LENGTH = 256.

defaultRole.description:
Optional. One of the roles can be set as default. The default role will have all new
cases automatically assigned to it. Note, there may not be any default
role, but the default role cannot be deleted once set. Once a role is set
as default the previous one will become non-default.

additionalRolesAccess.description:
Optional. Additional roles users associated with this role will have access to.
This will affect which cases they can see in the Google Security Operations
platform.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.
If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

totalSize.description: Total number of SocRoles.

Optional. The due time for the task in ms. When specified during task creation,

characters.

Optional. Associated case id (if task is related to a specific case)
Can be optional as tasks may exist independently or be associated with a
specific case.

A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page.

Case.description:
A Case represents a security investigation in SecOps. Cases group related
alerts and provide a central location for analysts to document their
findings, track progress, and collaborate on resolving security incidents.

Required. Case title, limited to 200 characters.
Replaces old property: Title.

Required. The stage of the Case. For example, "Triage", "Incident", "Investigation".

The default stage option is "Triage".

Required. Default value is HIGH.
Case priority. For example, "Informative", "Low", "Medium", "High",
"Critical".

Optional. Additional way to specify if the case marked as incident. The default is

Optional. This can be a user or a @SocRole, default value is the default soc-role

amount of data. During ingestion if the "alert package" crosses a specific

EnrichmentControl.description:
An EnrichmentControl resource represents a control to an enrichment.
Enrichment is the process of populating UDM events with more useful
information.
An enrichment control can have multiple records. Each record has a time range
and a description.
When an enrichment control is created for the first time, a new
EnrichmentControl resource is generated, along with an initial record whose
time range begins at the creation time. For subsequent creation requests for
the same control, the existing EnrichmentControl resource is returned, and
a new record with a start time of the current creation time is added.
Disabling an enrichment control involves updating the end time of the most
recent record associated with that EnrichmentControl resource to the
disabling time.

enrichmentControlOption.description: Required. The options to control the enrichment.

displayName.description: Optional. A human-readable name for this enrichment control.

description.description: Optional. A human-readable description for this enrichment control.

records.description: Output only. The records of the enrichment control.

EnrichmentControlOption.description: The options to control the enrichment.

enrichmentType.description: Required. The type of enrichment to be controlled.

enrichmentSource.description:
Required. The enrichment source that an enrichment is from. An enrichment source can
be either a log type or an external enrichment source that does not have a
log type.

EnrichmentControlRecord.description: The activity record of an enrichment control.

timeRange.description:
Output only. The time range of the events to apply the enrichment control. If the end
time is not set, it means the control will apply to all events after the
start time.

description.description: Output only. A human-readable description for this enrichment control record.

If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

DisableEnrichmentControlRequest.description: Request message to disable an enrichment control.

udmEnrichedFields.description:
A map of enriched fields in the parent event to information about where the
enriched data originated. The key of the map is the field path of the UDM
field. For example, "principal.user.windows_sid".
The value is a list of sources that the enriched data was taken from.
This is needed for on-the-fly Provenance where at read time we cannot
determine the enrichment source for a given field if more than one source
is present.

Feed.description: Feed is a resource that contains feed information needed to create a feed.

uid.description: Output only. Unique identifier for the feed.

displayName.description: Customer-provided feed name.

details.description:
Additional details of the feed, these details are dynamic and will be
different for each of the feeds.

state.description: Output only. State of the feed.
x-google-enum-descriptions:
Feed is configured and ready to ingest data. Newly created feeds have
this state. Once ingestion begins the feed will transition out of this
state and will not transition back.

Feed is Disabled. When a user disables a feed it will transition to this
state regardless of its current state. Once enabled a feed will
transition to its previous state.

Feed is enabled and currently ingesting data. A feed will transition to
this state from an ACTIVE or COMPLETED state when Chronicle has begun
fetching data for this feed.

Feed is enabled and has recently successfully ingested data. A feed will
transition to this state from RUNNING or FAILED once a fetch has
completed successfully.

Feed is enabled, but has recently failed to ingest data. A feed will
transition to this state only from RUNNING once a fetch has failed. It
will remain in this state until a subsequent fetch has succeeded.

failureMsg.description: Output only. Details about the most recent failure when feed state is FAILED.

readOnly.description: Output only. Whether this feed can be updated or deleted.

Output only. Failure details for the feed. If the feed is in the failure state,
this field will contain the details of the error cause and actions.

referenceId.description: Output only. Reference ID, this field will contain the legacy id of the feed.

FeedDetails.description:
Additional details of the feed, these details are dynamic and will be
different for each of the feeds.

The encoded private key. The string should be a private key in PEM format,
and should include the begin header and end footer lines. It may also
include newlines.

Example:
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
Proc-Type: 4,ENCRYPTED
DEK-Info: DES-EDE3-CBC,F23074E02CF47304


-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----

sslCertificate.description:
The encoded SSL certificate. The string should be an SSL certificate in
PEM format, and should include the begin header and end footer lines. It
may also include newlines.

Example:
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----

-----END CERTIFICATE-----

TrellixHxHostsSettings.description: Settings required by Feeds of TrellixHxHosts.

endpoint.description:
Required. Trellix HX Device URL.
This must be a valid URL with an http or https scheme. It has no default.
Usually a device URL is in the form of either:
https://xxx.trellix.com/hx/id//
 - or -
https://htapdeviceproxy.md.mandiant.net/dphb/hx//

TrellixStarXAuthentication.description:
TrellixStarXAuthentication contains a oneof with all of the authentication
types supported by Trellix *X devices.

MssoAuthentication.description:
Info for MssoAuthentication using a username, password, and login api
endpoint.

username.description:
Required. Username for MSSO authentication.
There are no restrictions on the format of the username. It has no default,
specifically enforced min / max length or character set. The username
will have been provided by an MSSO administrator and it is assumed that
they have provided a username that is internally consistent with MSSO
authentication requirements / validation.

password.description:
Required. Password of the account identified by username.
There are no restrictions on the format of the password. It has no default,
specifically enforced min / max length or character set. The password
will have been provided by an MSSO administrator and it is assumed that
they have provided a password that is internally consistent with MSSO
authentication requirements / validation.

apiEndpoint.description:
Required. The login api endpoint url.
This must be a valid URL with an http or https scheme. It has no default.

clientId.description:
Required. Client ID generated in Trellix IAM.
This is a unique identifier for the user that is generated in Trellix IAM.
It has no default, specifically enforced min / max length or character set.
It is assumed that the Client ID generated in Trellix IAM is internally
consistent with Trellix IAM authentication requirements / validation.

clientSecret.description:
Required. Secret associated with the Client ID.
This is the secret generated in Trellix IAM for the Client ID. It has no
default, specifically enforced min / max length or character set. It is
assumed that the secret generated in Trellix IAM is internally
consistent with Trellix IAM authentication requirements / validation.

scope.description:
Required. OAUTH 2 scope to request for the authentication token.
This is the OAUTH 2 scope to request for the authentication token. It has
no default, specifically enforced min / max length or character set. It is
assumed that the scope provided is internally consistent with Trellix IAM
authentication requirements / validation.

TrellixLocalAuthentication.description:
Info for TrellixLocalAuthentication using a username, password, endpoint,
and header name.

username.description:
Required. Username for Trellix Local authentication.
This is a unique username for the user that is generated on a Trellix
device. It has no default, specifically enforced min / max length,
or character set.

password.description:
Required. Password of the account identified by username.
There are no restrictions on the format of the password. It has no default,
specifically enforced min / max length or character set. The password
will have been provided by the Trellix administrator.

tokenEndpoint.description:
Required. The endpoint to fetch the token from.
This must be a valid URL with an http or https scheme. It has no default.

tokenHeader.description:
Required. The HTTP header name to use for the token for authentcated requests.
It varies per Trellix product. Refer to the Trellix API documentation for
the correct value. It has no default.

FeedFailureDetails.description:
FeedFailureDetails contains details about the errors thrown by chronicle for
the feeds. These are user visible details. These details help user identify
the root cause and take appropriate action for the feed errors.

errorCode.description:
Output only. error_code contains the error code for the feed. The field is populated for
the feeds with failed status.

httpErrorCode.description:
Output only. http_error_code contains the HTTP error code for the feed failure.
feed transfer failure may or may not result in http error code.

errorCause.description: Output only. error_cause contains the information regarding the failure cause.

errorAction.description:
Output only. error_action contains the user action prescribed for remediation of feed
error.

FeedPack.description:
FeedPack is a logical container for related LogTypes for which feeds can be
configured.

displayName.description: Output only. The display name of the feed pack.

description.description: Output only. The description of the feed pack.

icon.description: Output only. The icon image of the content pack in base64 in png format.

categories.description:
Output only. Categories the featured content is associated with. In case of product
feed packs, there will be only one category.

x-google-enum-descriptions: Use case based feed pack type.

packLogTypes.description: Output only. Log types featured in the pack.

hidden.description: Output only. Whether the feed pack should be displayed in the Feeds Page.

packDocumentation.description: Output only. Pack specific documentation in markdown format.

PackLogType.description: PackLogtype is a log type featured in the feed pack.

required.description: Whether the logtype is required to deploy a feed pack.

hasDefaultParser.description: Whether a default(prebuilt) parser is available for the given log type.

recommendedSourceType.description: The recommended source type for the log type.

additionalDocumentation.description: Documentation to share any CTAs for more reference.

FeedSourceTypeSchema.description:
Metadata that pertains to feed source types (see
Feed.FeedDetails.FeedSourceType) that is useful for building interfaces to
construct valid Feed messages.

name.description:

Format:
"projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/feedSourceTypeSchemas/{feed_source_type}".

displayName.description: A human-readable name for this feed source type.

description.description: A human-readable description for this feed source type.

readOnly.description:
Whether feeds having this source type, while they are expected to exist,
should not be modified (created, edited, or deleted).

feedSourceType.description:
The value to be used in the `details.feed_source_type` field in the Feed
message.

LogTypeSchema.description:
Metadata that pertains to a log type in the context of Feeds, and is useful
for building interfaces to construct valid Feed messages.

name.description:
The resource name.
Format:
"projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/feedSourceTypeSchemas/{feed_source_type}/logTypeSchemas/{log_type}"

displayName.description: A human-readable name for this log type.

logType.description: The log type to be used in the `details.log_type` field.

readOnly.description:
Whether this log type schema represents a feed configuration that may
exist, but should not be modified (created, edited, or deleted).

supportingDocumentation.description: A description that is displayed when users create or edit feeds.

detailsFieldSchemas.description:
The schemas for `detail` fields that are compatible with this log
type and feed source type. All fields for this log type and feed source
type are either contained here or within
details_field_schema_alternatives.

detailsFieldSchemaAlternatives.description:
There are sets of fields which represent alternatives to one another. A
user would only fill in one set of fields for any one alternative.

DetailsFieldSchema.description: A schema for a particular `details` field on the Feed proto message.

fieldPath.description:
The protocol buffer field path. For example,
"details.azure_ad_context_settings.authentication.token_endpoint".

displayName.description: A human-readable name for this field.

description.description: A human-readable description for this field.

type.description: A type that represents both the encoding and the semantics of this field.

enumFieldSchemas.description:
If a field has an enum type, this is the schema describing the possible
values.

required.description: Whether this field must be specified to have a valid feed configuration.

exampleInput.description:
For those fields with input requirements that aren't adequately expressed
by the SemanticType it is often useful to supply an example input.

readOnly.description: For those fields which are read only.

EnumFieldSchema.description:
A schema for a particular value, for a particular enum `details` field on
the Feed proto message.

value.description: The value to assign to the field.

displayName.description: A human-readable name for this value.

description.description: A human-readable description for this value.

DetailsFieldSchemaAlternative.description:
A collection of `details` field schema sets. The user must provide values
for only one set of fields within an alternative.

detailsFieldSchemaSets.description: The schemas for each alternative set of fields.

DetailsFieldSchemaSet.description: A collection of schemas for related fields within `details`.

displayName.description: A human-readable name for this collection of `details` fields.

description.description: A human-readable description for this collection of `details` fields.

detailsFieldSchemas.description: Schemas for the fields in this set.

httpBody.description: Required. The raw HTTP body is bound to this field.

secret.description: Immutable. The secret for the feed.

The generated secret. Store the secret value at a safe place and use it
while configuring your https push feed.

FindingsRefinement.description:
Represents a set of logic conditions used to refine various types of
findings such as curated rule detections.

displayName.description: Display name of the findings refinement.

type.description: The type of findings refinement.

createTime.description: Output only. The timestamp of when the findings refinement was created.

updateTime.description: Output only. The timestamp of when the findings refinement was last updated.

query.description:
The query for the findings refinement. Works in conjunction with the type
field to determine the findings refinement behavior. The syntax of this
query is the same as a UDM search string. See the following for more
information:
https://cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/investigation/udm-search

outcomeFilters.description:
Optional. The outcome filters for the findings refinement. These allow you to specify
filters that are applied to the outcome variables in the detection.
All filters must be true for a detection to match the findings refinement.

OutcomeFilter.description:
Outcome filter for the findings refinement. This is used to filter the
findings refinement based on the outcome variable values.

outcomeValue.description: Required. The value of the outcome variable to match.

outcomeFilterOperator.description: Required. The operator to be applied to the outcome variable.
x-google-enum-descriptions:
The outcome variable value must be equal to the outcome value in the
outcome filter.

The outcome variable value must contain the outcome value in the
outcome filter.

The outcome variable value must match the outcome value regex in the
outcome filter.

The outcome variable value must be a valid IP address in the outcome
filter value CIDR range.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page. If
this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

FindingsRefinementDeployment.description:
The FindingsRefinementDeployment resource represents the deployment state of
a findings refinement.

detectionExclusionApplication.description: The resources which the detection exclusion is applied to.

name.description:
Required. The resource name of the findings refinement deployment.
Format:
projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/findingsRefinements/{findings_refinement}/deployment

enabled.description:
Whether the findings refinement is currently deployed continuously against
incoming findings.

archived.description:
The archive state of the findings refinement deployment.
Cannot be set to true unless enabled is set to false.
If currently set to true, enabled cannot be updated to true.

updateTime.description: Output only. The timestamp when the findings refinement deployment was last updated.

DetectionExclusionApplication.description: Describes the detectors a detection exclusion is applied to.

deletedCuratedRuleSets.description:
Output only. The deleted CuratedRuleSets this detection exclusion applies to.
Indicates to the customer that the detection exclusion no longer applies
to the rule sets, so the detection exclusion should be updated.
Format:
projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/curatedRuleSetCategories/{category}/curatedRuleSets/{rule_set}

allFindingsRefinementDeployments.description: List of all findings refinement deployments.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page. If
this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

detectionExclusionApplication.description: The resources which the detection exclusion is applied to.

type.description:
Required. The type of findings refinement to test. This will affect the way the
query is evaluated.

query.description:
Required. The query for the findings refinement. Works in conjunction with the type
field to determine the findings refinement behavior that will be tested.
The syntax of this string is the same as a UDM search string. See the
following for more information:
https://cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/investigation/udm-search

outcomeFilters.description: Optional. The outcome filters for the findings refinement.

interval.description: Required. The time interval to test the findings refinement over.

activity.description: The tested activity for the finding refinement.

FindingsRefinementActivity.description: The activity for a specific findings refinement.

detectionExclusionActivity.description: The activity for the detection exclusion.

findingsRefinement.description:
Required. Full resource name for the findings refinement this activity corresponds
to.
Format:
projects/{project}/locations/{region}/instances/{instance}/findingsRefinements/{findings_refinement}

DetectionExclusionActivity.description:
The activity for a findings refinement that is a detection exclusion. The
activity is broken down per detector.

detectionExclusionDetectorActivities.description: The activity for the detection exclusion broken down by detector.

DetectionExclusionDetectorActivity.description:
The activity for a findings refinement that is a detection exclusion broken
down for one specific detector.

curatedRuleSet.description:
Full resource name for the curated rule set this activity corresponds
to. This field will only be set if the customer has access to the
curated rule set the exclusion is applied to.
Format:
projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/curatedRuleSetCategories/{curated_rule_set_category}/curatedRuleSets/{curated_rule_set}

deletedCuratedRuleSet.description:
Full resource name for the deleted curated rule set this activity
corresponds to. This field will only be set if the customer does
not have access to the curated rule set the exclusion is applied to.
Format:
projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/curatedRuleSetCategories/{curated_rule_set_category}/curatedRuleSets/{curated_rule_set}

excludedDetectionCount.description:
The number of detections for the detector that were excluded by the
detection exclusion.

totalDetectionCount.description:
The total number of detections found by the detector. This includes both
excluded detections and non-excluded detections.

interval.description: The time interval the activity is measured over.

activity.description: The activity for the findings refinement.

interval.description: The time interval the activity is measured over.

activities.description: The activities of all findings refinements.

Forwarder.description: Forwarder which sends data via Ingestion API.

displayName.description: User-specified forwarder name.

state.description: Output only. Current state of a Forwarder.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The default value. This value is used if the state is omitted.
Forwarder is allowed to upload data.
Forwarder is not allowed to upload data.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the Forwarder is created

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the Forwarder is updated

ForwarderConfig.description:
ForwarderConfig contains fields which manage the configuration of a given
Forwarder. Some settings, such as Metadata and Regex Filters, can interact
with individual Collector configurations.

uploadCompression.description: Determines if uploaded data will be compressed.

metadata.description:
Metadata applied at the Forwarder level. This can be used in conjunction
with, or be overridden by, Metadata applied at the Collector level. Refer
to each Metadata field for further information.

regexFilters.description:
RegexFilters applied at the Forwarder level, which can be overridden by
filters defined at the Collector level.

serverSettings.description: Server settings for load balancing and high availability config.

ForwarderMetadata.description: ForwarderMetadata can be applied at the Forwarder or Collector level.

assetNamespace.description:
Namespace is specified at the Forwarder and/or Collector level. If a
namespace is provided for both, the Collector's namespace takes precedence.

labels.description:
Arbitrary labels specified at the Forwarder and/or Collector level. If
both are provided, the labels are merged and the Collector's keys take
precedence over if the keys overlap.

RegexFilter.description:
RegexFilters can be applied at the Forwarder and/or Collector level. Filters
defined at both levels are merged, and the Collector's filters take
precedence if there are conflicting descriptions.

description.description: Describes what is being filtered and why.

regexp.description: The regular expression used to match against each incoming line.

behavior.description: Filter behavior to apply when a match is found.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Attempting to set this default value will result in a validation error.
Allow the filtered line to be uploaded.
Block the filtered line from being uploaded.

ServerSettings.description:
Server settings for load balancing and high availability configuration.
Default configuration values will be applied during resource creation for the
following fields when they are missing or zero-valued:
- graceful_timeout
- drain_timeout

enabled.description: Describes whether server functionality is enabled on the Forwarder.

gracefulTimeout.description:
A timeout value, truncated to seconds, after which the forwarder returns a
bad readiness/health check and still accepts new connections.

drainTimeout.description:
A timeout value, truncated to seconds, after which the forwarder waits for
active connections to successfully close on their own before being closed
by the server.

ForwarderHttpSettings.description:
HTTP settings for the Forwarder's server.
Default configuration values will be applied during resource creation for
the following fields when they are missing or zero-valued:
- port
- host
- read_timeout
- read_header_timeout
- write_timeout
- idle_timeout

port.description:
Port number that the HTTP server listens on for health checks from the
load balancer.

host.description:
IP address, or hostname that can be resolved to IP addresses, that the
server should listen on.

readTimeout.description:
Maximum amount of time, truncated to seconds, allowed to read the entire
request, both the header and the body.

readHeaderTimeout.description:
Maximum amount of time, truncated to seconds, allowed to read request
headers.

writeTimeout.description: Maximum amount of time, truncated to seconds, allowed to send a response.

idleTimeout.description:
Maximum amount of time, truncated to seconds, to wait for the next
request when idle connections are enabled.

routeSettings.description: Route-specific settings for the HTTP server.

RouteSettings.description:
User-configurable routes settings for the Forwarder's HTTP Server
Default configuration values will be applied during resource creation for
the following fields when they are missing or zero-valued:
- available_status_code
- ready_status_code
- unready_status_code

availableStatusCode.description:
Status code returned when a liveness check is received and the
forwarder is available.

readyStatusCode.description: Status code returned when it is ready to accept traffic.

unreadyStatusCode.description: Status code returned when it is not ready to accept traffic.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page. If
this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

config.description: Contents of file used by Forwarder containing config-related settings.

auth.description: Contents of file used by Forwarder containing API credential(s).

Collector.description: Collectors detail the configuration for a specific data type.

displayName.description: User-specified collector name.

state.description: Output only. State of the collector.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The default value. This value is used if the state is omitted.
Collector is allowed to accept data.
Collector is not allowed to accept data.

type.description: Output only. Type of the collector.

overrideTimezone.description:
Optional. Indicates if the timezone should be overridden, this field will be used for
self serve timezone override functionality and the logic is handled in the
RPC's

timeZone.description:
Optional. The timezone to override.this field will be used for self serve timezone
override functionality and the logic is handled in the RPC's ,We can't add
audit for this file as it is location field and we can't add audit for
location fields.

CollectorConfig.description:
CollectorConfig.
Default configuration values will be applied during resource creation for the
following fields when they are missing or zero-valued:
- max_seconds_per_batch
- max_bytes_per_batch

metadata.description:
Metadata applied at the Collector level. This can be used in conjunction
with, or take precedence over, Metadata applied at the Forwarder
level. Refer to each Metadata field for further information.

regexFilters.description:
Filters applied at the collector level, which can override filters defined
at the Forwarder level.

diskBuffer.description: Disk Buffering configuration at the collector level.

maxSecondsPerBatch.description: Maximum number of seconds between Forwarder batch uploads.

maxBytesPerBatch.description: Maximum number of bytes queued before Forwarder batch upload.

KafkaSettings.description:
KafkaSettings for a Collector.
Default configuration values will be applied during resource creation for the
following fields when they are missing or zero-valued:
- timeout

timeout.description: Kafka Timeout value, which will be truncated to seconds.

username.description: Username of an identity used for authentication.

password.description: Password of the account identified by username.

TLSSettings.description: TLS Settings information for Collectors with TLS support (e.g. Kafka, Syslog)

networkInterface.description: Interface to listen for PCAP data.

berkeleyPacketFilter.description: Berkeley Packet Filter (BPF) for PCAP handle.

SplunkSettings.description:
SplunkSettings for a Collector.
Default configuration values will be applied during resource creation for the
following fields when they are missing or zero-valued:
- minimum_window_size
- maximum_window_size

host.description: Host or IP for Splunk instance REST API.

port.description: Port associated with the Splunk instance, used with the host field.

minimumWindowSize.description: Minimum time range, truncated to seconds, for the Splunk query.

maximumWindowSize.description: Maximum time range, truncated to seconds, for the Splunk query.

queryString.description: Query used to filter records within Splunk.

queryMode.description:
Query mode for Splunk. Currently, only a value of "realtime" is supported,
For details about real-time searches in Splunk, please see the Splunk
documentation.

SyslogSettings.description:
SyslogSettings for a Collector.
Default configuration values will be applied during resource creation for the
following fields when they are missing or zero-valued:
- buffer_size
- connection_timeout

protocol.description: Syslog collector will listen using this protocol.
x-google-enum-descriptions: Attempting to set this default value will result in a validation error.
Syslog collector will listen for data using TCP.
Syslog collector will listen for data using UDP.

address.description:
IP Address or Hostname (will be resolved to an IP) on which the Forwarder's
syslog collector will listen.

port.description: Port on which the Forwarder's syslog collector will listen.

connectionTimeout.description:
The time, truncated to seconds, after which TCP connections will drop-off
after being inactive.

networkInterface.description: Interface to listen for WebProxy data.

berkeleyPacketFilter.description: Berkeley Packet Filter (BPF) for WebProxy handle.

DiskBuffer.description: DiskBuffering configuration applied at the collector level.

enabled.description: Describes whether disk buffering is enabled on the collector.

nextPageToken.description:
A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page. If
this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

inlineSource.description: StatsEvents to be imported are specified inline.

InlineSource.description: An import source with the events to import included inline.

eventBatchId.description: Required. Batch Id to use when ingesting StatsEvents.

IngestionStatsEvent.description: message to represent the stats related event forwarder will send.

agentStats.description: AgentStatsEvent generated by the collection agent.

eventTime.description: Required. The timestamp of the event.

inlineSource.description: Required. Entities to be imported are specified inline.

EntitiesInlineSource.description: An import source with the entities to import included inline.

logType.description: Required. The logtype of the log this entity is created from.

inlineSource.description: Required. Events to be imported are specified inline.

EventsInlineSource.description: An import source with the events to import included inline.

inlineSource.description: Required. Logs to be imported are specified inline.

hint.description: Optional. Opaque hint to help parsing the log.

LogsInlineSource.description: A import source with the logs to import included inline.

forwarder.description:
Optional. The forwarder sending the import request. This value determines the
SecOps CollectorID.

sourceFilename.description:
Optional. Source file name. Populated for certain types of files processed by the
outofband processor which may have metadata encoded in it for use by the
parser.

data.description: Required. Raw data for the log entry.

logEntryTime.description: Required. Timestamp of the log entry.

collectionTime.description:
Required. The time at which the log entry was collected. Must be after the
log_entry_time.

environmentNamespace.description:
Optional. The user-configured environment namespace to identify the data
domain the logs originated from. This namespace will be used as a tag to
identify the appropriate data domain for indexing and enrichment

A reference list.
Reference lists are user-defined lists of values which users can
use in multiple Rules.

displayName.description: Output only. The unique display name of the reference list.

revisionCreateTime.description: Output only. The timestamp when the reference list was last updated.

description.description: Required. A user-provided description of the reference list.

entries.description:
Required. The entries of the reference list.
When listed, they are returned in the order that was specified at creation
or update. The combined size of the values of the reference list may not
exceed 6MB.
This is returned only when the view is REFERENCE_LIST_VIEW_FULL.

During reference list creation, if this field is not set, the reference

user. For a scoped user, this field must be set. During reference list
update, if scope_info is requested to be updated, this field must be set.

ReferenceListEntry.description: An entry in a reference list.

value.description: Required. The value of the entry. Maximum length is 512 characters.

ScopeInfo.description: ScopeInfo specifies the scope info of the reference list.

referenceListScope.description:
Required. The list of scope names of the reference list, if the list is empty the
reference list is treated as unscoped.

Information about a UDM Search operation. This message is used to populate

The time when the operation results expire. This time may change while the
operation is in progress. If unset, the results never expire.

progress.description: A value from 0 to 1 representing the progress of the operation.

baselineEventsCount.description: The number of events matching the baseline query so far.

filteredEventsCount.description:
The number of events matching the snapshot query so far.
This is <= `baseline_events_count`. If the snapshot query is empty, this
will be equal to `baseline_events_count`.

baselineQuery.description: Query used to search for events.

snapshotQuery.description: Query used to filter the baseline query's events.

baselineTimeRange.description:
The time range used for the baseline query [inclusive start time,
exclusive end time).

snapshotTimeRange.description:
Time range used to filter the baseline query's events [inclusive start
time, exclusive end time). This time range is completely within
`baseline_time_range`. If not set, it is assumed to match
`baseline_time_range`.

caseInsensitive.description: If true, the search was performed in a case-insensitive manner.

statsRowsCount.description: The total number of rows returned for a stats query.

datatableWriteMetadata.description: Metadata about the DataTable write operation.

DataTableWriteMetadata.description: Metadata about a DataTable write operation.

error.description: The error result of the write operation in case of failure.

result.description: Metadata about the write operation's result in case of success.

datatableWriteOperationId.description: The operation ID of the write operation.

rowsCount.description: The number of rows written to the datatable.

state.description: Output only. The state of the write operation.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The operation is still in the queue and yet to be processed.
The system processed the operation successfully.
The system wasn't able to process the operation successfully.
The operation was cancelled by the system.

progressPercentage.description: Output only. The progress of the write operation.

GoogleCloudChronicleV1mainOperationMetad.description: Metadata for a Long Running Operation performed on Data Table Rows.

state.description: Output only. Current state of the operation.
x-google-enum-descriptions: The operation is still in the queue and yet to be processed.
The system processed the operation successfully.
The system wasn't able to process the operation successfully.
The operation was cancelled by the system.

progressPercentage.description: Output only. Progress Percentage of the operation, ranges from from 0.00 to 100.00.

LegacyFederatedUdmSearchViewResponse.description:
Response to a Federated UDM Search query. This will be used by
StreamSearchOperation RPC to return the results of a federated search
operation.

operation.description:
The name of the operation resource representing the UDM Search operation.
This can be passed to `StreamSearchOperation` to fetch stored results or
stream the results of an in-progress operation.

The metadata type of the operation is `UdmSearchMetadata`. The response
type is `LegacyFetchUdmSearchViewResponse`.

Format:
projects/{project}/locations/{location}/instances/{instance}/operations/{operation}

progress.description: Progress of the query represented as a double between 0 and 1.

tooManyEvents.description: If true, there are too many events to return and some have been omitted.

tooLargeResponse.description:
If true, the response to be returned to the UI is too large and some events
have been omitted.

complete.description: Streaming for this response is done. There will be no additional updates.

validBaselineQuery.description:
Indicates whether the request baseline_query is a valid structured query or
not. If not, `query_validation_errors` will include the parse error.

baselineEventsCount.description: The number of events in the baseline query.

validSnapshotQuery.description:
Indicates whether the request baseline and snapshot queries are valid. If
not, `query_validation_errors` will include the parse error.

queryValidationErrors.description: Parse error for the baseline_query and/or the snapshot_query.

filteredEventsCount.description:
The number of events in the snapshot that match the snapshot_query.
This is <= `baseline_events_count`. If the snapshot query is empty this
will be equivalent to `baseline_events_count`.

events.description:
List of UDM events.
NOTE: After complete is set to true, the `UdmEventList` message will be
omitted from the response. The latest message should be used as reference.
If the `UdmEventList` message is returned again, then it should replace the
previous value.

fieldAggregations.description: List of UDM fields with aggregated values.

groupedFieldAggregations.description: List of grouped fields with aggregated values.

statsFunctionParameterValidationErrorMes.description:
If the request's stats_function_parameter` is invalid, this field will
contain the detailed error message.

stats.description: Stats results when the query is for statistics

dataSources.description: Datasource of the query and results in case of a statistics query

x-google-enum-descriptions: To be used for detections data source.
To be used for ruleset with detections datasource.

aiOverview.description:
AI generated overview for the search results. Only populated if the AI
features are enabled for the customer and generate_ai_overview is set to
true in the request.

activityTimeline.description:
Timeline of event counts broken into hourly/daily buckets to identify
activity.

timeline.description:
Timeline of event counts broken into buckets.
This populates the trend-over-time chart in the UI.

instanceUdmSearchResponses.description: All the instance specific UDM search responses.

instanceAggregations.description:
Instance aggregations for the search results.
Provides information on the number of events per instance for the
aggregations panel in the UI. This allows users to easily drill-down into
the individual instances.

detectionFieldAggregations.description:
List of detection fields with aggregated values.
This enabled filtering in the Alerts panel for federated search.

detectionInstanceAggregations.description:
Instance aggregations for the detection search results.
Provides information on the number of detections per instance for the
detections aggregations panel in the UI. This allows users to easily
drill-down into the individual instances.

selectedFields.description: Fields and variables that have been selected in the query.

unselectedFields.description: Fields and variables that have been unselected / excluded in the query.

totalEventsLimit.description:
Limit on the total number of events returned by the search. This is needed
in the UI to show in the Result Summary panel since the logic to determine
the limit is in the backend.

instanceEventsLimit.description:
Limit on the number of events returned by each instance. This is needed in
the UI to show in the Result Summary panel since the logic to determine the
limit is in the backend.

baselineTimedEntitiesCount.description: Output only. The number of timed entities in the baseline query.

filteredTimedEntitiesCount.description:
Output only. The number of events in the snapshot that match the snapshot_query.
This is <= `baseline_timed_entities_count`. If the snapshot query is empty
this will be equivalent to `baseline_timed_entities_count`.

degradedResponse.description:
Indicates degraded response sent to the client.
Currently, this is set to true when memcache health check fails and we
serve partial results from spanner.

Collection.description:
Collection represents a container of objects (such as events, entity
context metadata, detection finding metadata) and state (such as
investigation details).

An example use case for Collection is to model a detection and investigation
from detection finding metadata to investigative state collected in the
course of the investigation. For more complex investigation and response
workflows a Collection could represent an incident consisting of multiple
child findings or incidents. This can be expanded on to model remediation
elements of a full detection and response workflow.

NEXT TAG: 25

id.description:
Unique ID for the collection.
The ID is specific to the type of collection. For example, with rule
detections this is the detection ID.

type.description: What the collection represents.
x-google-enum-descriptions: An alert reported in customer telemetry.
A finding from the Uppercase team.
A detection found by applying a rule.
An alert generated by Chronicle machine learning models.
An alert coming from other SIEMs via Chronicle SOAR.

idNamespace.description: The ID namespace used for the Collection.
x-google-enum-descriptions: A normalized telemetry event from Google Security Command Center.
An alert coming from other SIEMs via Chronicle SOAR.

lastUpdatedTime.description: Time the collection was last updated.

timeWindow.description: Time interval that the collection represents.

collectionElements.description:
Constituent elements of the collection. Each element shares an association
that groups it together and is a component of the overall collection. For
example, a detection collection may have several constituent elements that
each share a correlation association that together represent a particular
pattern or behavior.

detection.description:
Detection metadata for findings that represent detections, can include
rule details, machine learning model metadata, and indicators implicated
in the detection (using the .about field).

detectionTime.description:
Timestamp within the time_window related to the time of the
collection_elements. For Rule Detections, this timestamp is the end of the
the time_window for multi-event rules or the time of the event for single
event rules. For late-arriving events that trigger new alerts, the
detection_time will be the event time of the event.

investigation.description:
Consolidated investigation details (categorization, status, etc) typically
for collections that begin as detection findings and then evolve with
analyst action and feedback into investigations around the detection
output.

tags.description: Tags set by UC/DSML/RE for the Finding during creation.

responsePlatformInfo.description: Alert related info of this same alert in customer's SOAR platform.

feedbackSummary.description:
The current primary analyst feedback.
This does not include the history of feedback given, which may be supplied
in `feedback`.

feedbackHistory.description:
The history of feedback submitted by analysts for this finding,
in descending order by timestamp.
This field is limited to the most recent 1000 feedback events.
The primary feedback will also be included in this list.

soarAlert.description: A boolean field indicating that the alert is present in SOAR.

soarAlertMetadata.description: Metadata fields of alerts coming from other SIEM systems via SOAR.

dataAccessScope.description: The resource name of the DataAccessScope of this collection.

detectionTimingDetails.description:
Detection timing details for the collection. These details are used to
determine prossible causes of latency for the detection.
This field is only set for detections that are generated by rules.

x-google-enum-descriptions: Detection is generated by a reprocessing run.
Detection is generated by a retrohunt run.

latencyMetrics.description:
The latency metrics for the specific detection. These metrics are
calculated from ALL of the events that contribute to the detection, not
just the sampled ones.

ruleRunFrequency.description: The run frequency of the rule when it generated the detection.

simulatedEventCount.description:
The total number of simulated events that contributed to this detection.
Simulated events are realistic threat sequences (Raw Logs or UDM)
programmatically delivered into the production ingestion pipeline to verify
the entire detection lifecycle—from identification to action.

simulatedEventNames.description:
The set of all values from event ingestion_labels where SIMULATED is set as
the key, for all simulated events that participated in this detection.

association.description:
Metadata that provides the relevant association for the references in the
element. For a detection, this can be the correlated aspect of the
references that contributed to the overall detection. For example, may
include sub-rule condition, machine learning model metadata, and/or
indicators implicated in this component of the detection
(using the .about field).

references.description:
References to model primatives including events and entities that share a
common association.
Even though a reference can have both UDM and entity, a collection of
references (of a single element) will only have one type of message in it
(either UDM / Entity).

label.description: A name that labels the entire references group.

referencesSampled.description:
Copied from the detection event_sample.too_many_event_samples field.
If true, the number of references will be capped at the sample limit
(set at rule service).
This is applicable to both UDM references and Entity references.

latencyMetrics.description:
Latency metrics for the specific element. These are
calculated from all the contributing events or entities for a single event
variable, not just the sampled ones included in references. This is
currently only populated for UDM events.

Reference.description:
Reference to model primatives including event and entity. As support is added
for fast retrieval of objects by identifiers, this will be
expanded to include ID references rather than full object copies.

event.description:
Only one of event or entity will be populated for a single
reference.
Start one-of
Event being referenced.

entity.description:

BaseOperation.description:
This resource represents a long-running operation that is the result of a
network API call.

name.description:
The server-assigned name, which is only unique within the same service that
originally returns it. If you use the default HTTP mapping, the
`name` should be a resource name ending with `operations/{unique_id}`.

done.description:
If the value is `false`, it means the operation is still in progress.
If `true`, the operation is completed, and either `error` or `response` is
available.

error.description: The error result of the operation in case of failure or cancellation.

Operation.description:
This resource represents a long-running operation that is the result of a
network API call.

metadata.description:
Service-specific metadata associated with the operation.  It typically
contains progress information and common metadata such as create time.
Some services might not provide such metadata.  Any method that returns a
long-running operation should document the metadata type, if any.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

response.description:
The normal, successful response of the operation.  If the original
method returns no data on success, such as `Delete`, the response is
`google.protobuf.Empty`.  If the original method is standard
`Get`/`Create`/`Update`, the response should be the resource.  For other
methods, the response should have the type `XxxResponse`, where `Xxx`
is the original method name.  For example, if the original method name
is `TakeSnapshot()`, the inferred response type is
`TakeSnapshotResponse`.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

The rule's match window is longer than the time range it's being tested
over.  Returns the match window duration and testing interval duration as
metadata labeled `match_window_duration` and `testing_interval_duration`,
formatted as Go durations.

Example ErrorInfo:

   { "reason": "RULE_TESTING_INTERVAL_TOO_SHORT",
     "domain": "chronicle.googleapis.com",
     "metadata": {
       "match_window_duration": "24h",
       "testing_interval_duration": "12h",
     }
   }

     "domain": "chronicle.googleapis.com",
